We are seeking a Technical Project Manager to lead and coordinate projects within the high-performance Solana ecosystem. The ideal candidate will drive the development and implementation of decentralized exchange solutions, ensuring seamless execution and integration within the DEX spac
data.jd_text:
**Summary:**
We are seeking a Technical Project Manager to lead and coordinate projects within the high-performance Solana ecosystem. The ideal candidate will drive the development and implementation of decentralized exchange solutions, ensuring seamless execution and integration within the DEX space.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Coordinate and oversee all stages of project management, from initiation to closure, within the Solana ecosystem.
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure timely delivery of projects and alignment with strategic objectives.
* Identify project risks and develop mitigation strategies to ensure project success.
* Communicate project status, milestones, and outcomes to stakeholders effectively.
* Implement best practices and tools for project management and tracking within the decentralized exchange landscape.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
* Proven experience in project management within the blockchain or decentralized finance (DeFi) industry.
* Strong understanding of the Solana ecosystem and decentralized exchanges.
* Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to work in a fast-paced environment.
* Strong communication and leadership skills to effectively manage teams and stakeholders.
* Project Management Professional (PMP) certification is a plus.

data.title: Senior Project Manager (Tech)
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Description**
**Position Overview**
The Senior Project Manager (Tech) will support the Technical Team Lead in overseeing internal AI and software development projects, as well as client-facing technical solutions. The role will focus on end-to-end project delivery, including planning, executio
data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
**Position Overview**
The Senior Project Manager (Tech) will support the Technical Team Lead in overseeing internal AI and software development projects, as well as client-facing technical solutions. The role will focus on end-to-end project delivery, including planning, execution, monitoring, and closure, with an emphasis on agile methodologies. This position requires strong organizational skills, technical background in software development, and the ability to manage cross-functional teams effectively.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**

* Manage the full project lifecycle from initiation to delivery, ensuring projects are completed on time, within scope, and aligned with objectives.
* Support resource planning, workload allocation, and timeline estimation for multiple concurrent projects.
* Track progress, risks, and dependencies across cross-functional teams and ensure timely reporting to the Technical Team Lead.
* Apply agile methodologies to manage sprint cycles, prioritize deliverables, and accommodate ad-hoc requests during project execution.
* Coordinate integration efforts and R&D-driven development for both internal and client-facing solutions.
* Identify and propose process improvements to enhance efficiency and delivery quality.
* Create, contribute to, and maintain department Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and other technical documentation.
* Maintain accurate project documentation and reporting using ClickUp.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
* 3+ years of experience in project management, with a focus on full-stack software development.
* Proven experience managing cross-functional teams in agile environments.
* Strong knowledge of project management tools, specifically ClickUp, or alternatively JIRA and Confluence.
* Familiarity with AI/ML projects is a plus.
* Exceptional organizational, problem-solving, and communication skills.

**Key Competencies**

* Adaptability and agility in dynamic environments.
* Strong stakeholder management and reporting skills.
* Crisis-handling ability under tight deadlines.
* Experience working in cross-cultural, distributed teams.

**Careers at APCO**
Through our recruitment, hiring and retention efforts, APCO is committed to building teams to bring the best possible expertise and robust perspectives to our clients. We embrace differences in experience, viewpoint and ideas, starting with our values and extending to our behaviors, policies and practices. We aim to create a positive work experience and welcoming environment where everyone feels they belong and can thrive.

strong PM Agile coach with Data and Analytics background

Establish and maintain program governance, reporting, and communication
  
  
Engage with senior stakeholders and provide executive-level updates

Support change management and adoption activities

Proven experience as a Program Manager delivering complex programs
  
  
Strong project and program governance expertise
  
  
Excellent stakeholder, communication, and leadership skills

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Technology, or related field
  
  
10+ years of overall experience with 4+ years in program management roles (preferred)

**Description**
Network Losses (NL) Organization exists to reduce and control operationally controllable defects that results into financial loss to the company presented in inventory overwrite or customer concessions.
  
  
NL AM -Network losses Area Manager- will be responsible for reducing operations defects leading to network losses.
  
  
NL AM will work closely & daily with field operations, security and loss prevention, legal, vendor management teams with to make sure process defects leading to losses are eliminated and reflected into goodness is reflected at weekly loss figures.
  
  
Key job responsibilities
  
  
Accountable for achieving the overarching Network Losses (NL) metrics targets through:
  
  

* Collaborating with operational stakeholders to align new input metrics.
* Daily tracking of input metrics, actively engaging with operations to implement fixes and enhance these metrics.
* Identifying and addressing new operational defects, implementing effective countermeasures.
* Identifying and highlighting structural challenges to drive the NL charter forward.
* Drive NL Weekly business review.
* Daily useage of SQL to generate business insights

**Basic Qualifications**

* 1+ years of employee and performance management experience
* Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or 2+ years of Amazon (blue badge/FTE) experience
* Work a flexible schedule/shift/work area, including weekends, nights, and/or holidays
* Can push and pull wheeled dollies loaded with products up to 100 pounds during shifts
* Experience with advanced use of SQL for data mining and business intelligence
* 6+ years of performance metrics, process improvement or lean techniques experience
* Six Sigma Black Belt Certification

**Preferred Qualifications**

* 1+ years of performance metrics, process improvement or lean techniques experience
* Bachelor's degree in business, engineering, operations, supply chain, transportation logistics

The Project Manager, Marketing provides operational and coordination support to the Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) and the wider marketing team. The role ensures efficient management of incoming requests, smooth coordination across teams and departments, and effective execution of marketing related projects and activities.
  
  
Requirements
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**

* Serve as the primary coordination point for incoming queries and requests directed to the Marketing function and the CMO.
* Manage and prioritize day to day operational tasks and follow ups for the CMO.

* Manage the CMO’s calendar including scheduling meetings, coordinating appointments, and ensuring appropriate prioritization of engagements.
* Prepare meeting agendas, briefing materials, and document meeting minutes including tracking of key actions and decisions.
* Support planning, coordination, and monitoring of marketing projects and initiatives.
* Ensure transparency, alignment, and timely follow up on deliverables across teams.

**Key Skills and Competencies**

* Strong project coordination and organizational skills
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast paced environment

* Strong attention to detail and follow through
* Ability to work independently and maintain high levels of discretion and professionalism

Benefits
  
  
• 24 annual leave days
  
• Annual flight home
  
• Life insurance plan
  
• Medical insurance plan (with the option to upgrade at your own cost)
  
• Bonus scheme (in relevant departments)
  
• Invitations to participate in various company functions, staff events, and department team building events

data.title: Lead Data Intelligence Project Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**About Us**
At Dyson, we’re driven by a relentless pursuit of innovation—pushing boundaries in engineering, AI, and robotics. Our new Data Intelligence team sits at the heart of this mission: shaping Dyson’s future through data. Here, we blend creativity, precision, and audacity to power intelligen
data.jd_text:
**About Us**
At Dyson, we’re driven by a relentless pursuit of innovation—pushing boundaries in engineering, AI, and robotics. Our new Data Intelligence team sits at the heart of this mission: shaping Dyson’s future through data. Here, we blend creativity, precision, and audacity to power intelligent products. We craft data strategies and pipelines that fuel the next generation of connected devices.
  
  
You’ll work alongside brilliant minds from Dyson global engineering team and external software/hardware partners in an environment built for exploration, discovery, delivery and impact.
  
  
**About The Role**
As the Lead Data Intelligence Project Manager, you ensure that every project has the resources it needs to take off, you'll orchestrate data operations that power product development across Dyson’s global teams. Key responsibilities include:
  
  

* Act as the primary liaison between our external project teams and our internal technical departments (Data Curation and MLOps). You will own the "data logistics" for the entire project lifecycle—from initial storage setup to final delivery of high-quality training sets.
* You will be the strategic lead for our labelling workforce, managing the balance between our in-house contractors and 3rd party service providers to ensure we never hit a capacity bottleneck.
* Overseeing data storage setup and maintenance to guarantee security, accessibility, and compliance.
* Maintaining rigorous documentation and workflows that ensure project transparency and repeatability.
* Supporting cross-functional communication between data teams, contractors, and engineering partners.

**About you**

* 8+ years in Project or Program Management, with at least 2 years specifically in AI/ML Data Operations or Data Management. You’ve delivered complex projects on time and within scope—often in fast-changing settings.
* Demonstrated success in managing 3rd party vendors or service providers, including contract negotiation, quality assurance, issue resolution, and ongoing relationship management.
* Machine Learning Lifecycle Knowledge: Strong understanding of how data flows from collection → cleaning → labeling → training → deployment.
* Tooling Familiarity: Experience with labeling platforms (Labelbox, CVAT, Label Studio) and project management tools (Jira, Asana, or Airtable).
* Data Literacy: Basic understanding of data storage architectures (S3, SQL, Snowflake) and data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA).
* Communication: Exceptional "translation" skills—the ability to explain technical MLOps constraints to business stakeholders and vice versa.
* A solid understanding of modern data storage solutions (cloud, on-premises, hybrid), including principles of data security, privacy, access control, and regulatory compliance.
* Able to translate complex technical requirements into clear actions for both technical and non-technical audiences. You’re adept at influencing and building consensus.
* Confident interfacing with cross-functional teams—coordinating with data scientists, engineers, product managers, and external stakeholders to deliver smooth handovers and alignment.
* Well-versed in project management tools and methodologies (e.g., Agile, Scrum, or Waterfall). Known for driving continuous improvement, clear documentation, and repeatable processes.
* Thorough, highly organized, and relentless in troubleshooting issues and removing blockers before they impact delivery.
* Proactive, accountable, and comfortable juggling competing priorities. You always keep end goals in focus and balance urgency with quality.
* Bachelor’s degree in a relevant discipline (e.g., Computer Science, Data Engineering, Engineering, or Information Technology); higher degree or PMP/Prince2 certification is a plus.

Dyson is an equal opportunity employer. We know that great minds don’t think alike, and it takes all kinds of minds to make our technology so unique. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and employment decisions are made without regard to race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran status or other any other dimension of diversity.

RH Aero Systems is setting the standard for aviation support equipment and services. Through our industry-leading businesses – Rhinestahl and HYDRO – we deliver capability across custom-designed Ground Support Equipment, OEM-licensed engine and airframe tooling, 26 global service centers, and innovative engineered solutions for OEMs, MROs, and Operators worldwide.

* Provide leadership to the teams to ensure product quality, reliability, and delivery of commitments on time and on budget.
* Oversee and expand service business in the region through operational efficiencies, on-time delivery, and customer experience.
* Drive team performance and collaboration to strengthen relationships with both internal and external stakeholders.
* Lead and inspire team leads to create and execute strategic plans aligned with their respective functions and targets.
* Provide regular reports and strategic recommendations to the leadership team.
* Identify and implement business improvements that enhance cost-efficiency and operational effectiveness.
* Clearly communicate the company’s value propositions and ensure alignment with organizational processes.
* Proactively assess and address challenges, ensuring timely resolution of issues impacting operations or strategy.
* Lead and support change initiatives that foster innovation and continuous improvement across the organization.

Daniel Geiger

Ahfeldstraße 10 ▪ 77781 Biberach / Baden

data.title: Project Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Description**
With more than 14000 people around the globe, GHD is one of the world’s leading professional services companies operating in the global markets of water, energy and resources, environment, property and buildings, and transportation. Serving clients across five continents and the
data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
With more than 14000 people around the globe, GHD is one of the world’s leading professional services companies operating in the global markets of water, energy and resources, environment, property and buildings, and transportation. Serving clients across five continents and the Pacific region, GHD people share a passion for exceeding the expectations of our clients and contributing to their success.
  
  
If you want to start a career with impact, being involved in solving everyday problems through to tackling some of the great challenges our clients and communities face, you’ll find that here at GHD.
  
  
We're looking for a
**Project Manager**
to join our dynamic team and be accountable for one or more medium-sized projects of low to moderate complexity with a small- to medium-sized team. This role will focus on the creation of the project vision and planning documents and manage the implementation, reporting, and performance of the overall project with the expectation of delivering on time, within budget, and to scope.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Document Preparation: Prepare moderately complex documents using a variety of applications for technology devices, such as standard office software. Also responsible for gathering and summarizing data for reports.
* Stakeholder Management: Plan and deliver stakeholder engagement activities to develop effective working relationships for projects and to ensure that stakeholder needs and concerns are identified and met.
* Personal Capability Building: Develop own capabilities by participating in assessment and development planning activities as well as formal and informal training and coaching; gain or maintain external professional accreditation, where relevant, to improve performance and fulfill personal potential. Maintain an understanding of relevant technology, external regulation, and industry best practices through ongoing education, attending conferences, and reading specialist media.
* Continuous Improvement: Contribute to reviewing existing operations in own area of work, and generate new ideas to assist in identifying continuous improvements.
* Work Scheduling and Allocation: Assign short-term work schedules to a team of subordinates in order to achieve expectations while following established timelines.
* Project Assurance: Conduct assurance reviews within the project, facilitate the delivery of assurance reviews by independent third parties, and take appropriate action to resolve any issues identified, so that senior stakeholders have confidence that the project can deliver according to time, budget, and quality.
* Project Scope Definition: Manage the delivery of research, workshops, and other activities to support the specification and agreement of project deliverables.
* Project Planning: Produce workstream or project plans, ensuring that all activities are identified, are appropriately organized to deliver project objectives, and comply with the organization's project management framework.
* Project Reporting and Review: Draft project review reports and presentations, including key information, commentary, and recommendations, to support the review process and enable stakeholders to evaluate progress and agree on change.
* Project Resource Management: Manage the deployment of project resources, proactively managing project costs, providing forecasts, and presenting variances with narrative at appropriate review points to ensure effective utilization.
* Project Risk and Issue Management: Identify and evaluate risks, issues, dependencies, and constraints associated with the project, escalating matters where appropriate. Where necessary, develop, agree on, and implement solutions to overcome these concerns.
* Project Closeout and Handover: Draft elements of product documentation and user training to support handover to business as usual. Contribute to post-project reviews and identification of lessons learned.
* Project Team Management: Lead a small project team; communicate the necessary outcomes and the direction required to achieve them; coordinate team actions on project tasks; request additional team member support when needed; and identify areas of improvement to build the capability of the team through training, coaching, and mentoring.
* Requirements Management: Coordinate the identification and elicitation of requirements; conduct analysis of those requirements for completion and alignment; document and manage requirements throughout the life of the project; and coordinate the verification of the end deliverable. Generally done at the level of project phases.

**Experience**

* General Experience: Experience enables job holder to deal with the majority of situations and to advise others (Over 3 years to 6 years)
* Managerial Experience: Experience of general supervision of more junior colleagues (7 to 12 months)

**Take on some of the world's toughest challenges- with everyone at GHD backing you every step of the way.**
**At GHD, we celebrate the power of a multicultural workforce and champion individual achievement. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to providing fair and inclusive employment opportunities to all qualified candidates and employees—regardless of background, identity, or experience. We believe that diversity drives innovation, and we welcome the unique perspectives that each team member brings to our organization.**

data.title: Senior Project Manager
data.raw_snippet: STRUCTURAL integrates technology-driven solutions with specialty contracting services to improve, protect, and enhance the existing infrastructure of owners directly, and in partnership with designers and contractors. STRUCTURAL offers a wide range of specialty repair and maintenance services and is
data.jd_text:
STRUCTURAL integrates technology-driven solutions with specialty contracting services to improve, protect, and enhance the existing infrastructure of owners directly, and in partnership with designers and contractors. STRUCTURAL offers a wide range of specialty repair and maintenance services and is the largest concrete repair contractor in the United States, with roughly 2,500 employees working from locations nationwide and in select international markets. STRUCTURAL is the open-shop contracting business line of Structural Group.
  
  
STRUCTURAL integrates technology-driven solutions with specialty contracting services to improve, protect, and enhance the existing infrastructure of owners directly, and in partnership with designers and contractors. STRUCTURAL offers a wide range of specialty repair and maintenance services and is the largest concrete repair contractor in the United States, with roughly 4,500 employees working from locations nationwide and in select international markets. STRUCTURAL has been established in UAE since 2008 and has offices/operations in Qatar and KSA.
  
  
We are currently recruiting for a Senior Project Manager to be based in our Dubai office supporting projects throughout the UAE. As a Senior Project Manager for STRUCTURAL, you will be responsible for managing complex construction projects that make the structures of the UAE and GCC stronger and last longer.
  
  
**As a Successful Candidate, You Will Be Responsible For**

* Managing self-performed complex repair and restoration projects
* Preparing contracts and negotiating revisions while working with internal contracts teams as needed
* Developing and managing schedules in collaboration with field leadership
* Maintaining profit & loss responsibility as well as other project financials including projections, etc.
* Providing strong leadership, mentorship and supervision to Project Managers, Field Managers, Project Engineers, and subcontractors
* Working closely with in-house Estimators and Sales teams to assist in reviewing bids and developing proposals.
* Evaluating risk and loss exposure on projects managed
* Developing and maintaining strong customer relationships
* Demonstrating dedication to safety and quality control on all projects

Candidates who meet the following criteria may be considered for this exciting position:
  
  

* Excellent written and verbal English communication and technical writing
* Bachelor’s degree in Civil/Structural Engineering/construction management or related field of study
* 12+ years of relevant experience within the Commercial construction and / or restoration industry
* Ability to speak and write in Arabic preferred
* Demonstrated capability to successfully manage construction projects.
* Previous experience managing projects that include structural concrete repair, waterproofing, façade repair and/or historic restoration.
* Proven knowledge of computer-based programs including Microsoft Office as well as project management and forecasting tools
* Strong leadership skills to effectively train and mentor others.
* The ability to work effectively with a team
* Capable of managing multiple projects and ensuring successful and on-time delivery
* Job location is in the UAE with flexibility to travel within the GCC

Our ideal candidate is an innovative but decisive individual who can work effectively in a highly collaborative, team-based environment; has the ability to set goals and expectations and hold others accountable; can encourage and mentor others; is approachable, empathetic and outgoing; can quickly gain trust and respect; and is able to establish and maintain relationships.
  
  
STRUCTURAL is proud of a company culture that promotes 24/7 safety and quality. We offer competitive compensation and benefits including medical and dental insurance, 401(k), paid holidays, vacation, tuition reimbursement, career development and growth opportunities, and a caring work environment.
  
  
STRUCTURAL is committed to a Safety 24/7 culture and offers competitive compensation and benefits including medical, dental insurance, 401(k), paid holidays & vacation, tuition reimbursement, career development and growth opportunities, and a caring work environment. EOE/M/F/D/V

data.jd_text:
****Contexte et environnement****
**Internal & External**
Types of contacts associated with the position:
  
  
**Context:**

* The position is an important link between the users and suppliers for key goods and services requirements.
* Requires very high commitment to ensure supply of goods and services as it involves activities which directly impact the affiliate operations.

**Internal:**

* Interaction with MD, VP’s, Managers & Supervisors from other departments in UAE & Qatar & KAZ + Global Procurement Office in Paris + Other TE Affiliates Worldwide and BCP teams supply chain

**External:**

* External Interactions with suppliers .

Framework for the position's reflections
  
  

* Implement instructions by optimizing processes
* Strict implementation of instructions

Nature of the position's interactions
  
  

* :The position requires precise communication skills
* The position requires the ability to influence and convince
* The position requires negotiation skills

Position's operations
  
  

* Operations in UAE + Qatar + KAZ; highly competitive markets

****Activités****
SUPPLY CHAIN CRISIS MANAGEMENT
  
  

* Support VP-Aftersales in crisis organization and determine new supply scheme including follow-up of shipments and set up of new route including all the HSSEQ standards’s application.
* Support VP-Aftersales in Kazakstan to reduce the current stock quantities and reduce storage to 1 location
* Launch tender with VP-Aftersales and Logistic director in KAZ for new warehousing and logistic provider
* Support VP-Aftersales in crisis allocation of products and set up daily KPI on product’s delivery, costs, …

CATEGORY LEAD (PROCUREMENT)
  
  

* Support procurement activities for TEMME & TEMQ across assigned categories: Logistics and Warehousing.
* Support the Procurement Manager in implementation of purchasing and sourcing strategies and contribute inputs to category and sourcing initiatives.
* Support the analysis and review of purchasing data to help identify opportunities for cost optimization, synergies, and process improvements.
* Assist in the end ‑ to ‑ end tender process, including bid preparation, coordination with stakeholders, evaluation support, and documentation, up to contractual finalization.
* Participate in tender processes, negotiations, tender committees, and contract preparation activities as required, under supervision.
* Provide operational support to stakeholders in supplier management activities following contract award.
* Maintain professional and constructive working relationships with internal customers and suppliers, ensuring timely communication and follow‑up.
* Act in accordance with the organization’s standards on business ethics, compliance, HSSEQ, and sustainability, demonstrating professionalism and integrity in all activities.

PURPOSES
  
  

* Ensure that purchases are made within the budgeted value per the purchasing plan, in accordance with the purchasing procedures.
* Ensure the timely availability of goods and services to the end users.
* Respect deadlines and financial targets.
* Adapt the supply chain crisis mode with clear KPI and streamline communication

**Compliance Objectives:**

* Ensure full adherence to the company’s compliance program, encompassing economic sanctions and export control program, anti-corruption and fraud policies, procedures, and the Code of Conduct in all professional activities.
* Promptly notify the compliance officer/economic sanctions referent to any instances of noncompliance or breaches of economic sanctions or export control that comes to my attention. Any other compliance risks identified during day-to-day activities should also be reported immediately.

**HSSEQ Objectives**

* Strictly comply with TEMME HSSEQ policies, procedures, and applicable regulations at all times.
* Participate in HSSEQ training, promote HSSEQ awareness, and support knowledge sharing when required.
* Promptly report incidents, near misses, hazards, and injuries in line with HSSEQ requirements.
* Participate in safety tours, incident investigations, and follow ‑ up actions as required.
* Adhere to vehicle management and sustainability requirements, with the authority to stop unsafe operations.
* Demonstrate exemplary professionalism, vigilance, and commitment to HSSEQ in all activities

****Profil du candidat****
**Qualification**

* Minimum Master degree, preferably from the following disciplines: Business Economics, Engineering, Manufacturing, Procurement and Supply Chain Management.

**Technical Skills**

* Preferred - Experienced in the use of ERP systems & MS Suite (SAP, Ariba, Excel, Word, Power Point, Power BI).
* Required - High proficiency in MS Office for data analysis. Basis legal knowledge to read and understand agreements.

**Soft Skills**

* Strong negotiation and commercial awareness, with the ability to anticipate issues and support strategic decision‑making.
* Solid professional skills, including project coordination, compliance with business policies, and strong PC literacy.
* Good influencing and communication skills, with the ability to support discussions, facilitate collaboration, and contribute to change initiatives.
* Clear and effective communicator, able to engage constructively with stakeholders at different levels of the organization.
* Customer‑service-oriented team player with a collaborative mindset.
* Results‑focused, proactive, and self‑motivated.

**Knowledge**

* Experience supporting supply market research, supplier identification and qualification, tender processes, contract management, and performance tracking.
* Proficient in the use of procurement tools and systems, including spend analysis, e‑procurement platforms, electronic tendering, e‑auctions, and contract management tools.

**Language Skills**

* English level: fluent

**Experience**

* Mandatory 10+ years’ experience in Procurement with supply chain complexity, with exposure to strategic sourcing or category management.
* Experience supporting complex tenders, contracts, and supplier management.
* Strong working knowledge of logistics procurement (Incoterms, HS Codes, local regulations, HSSEQ)
* Experience supporting stakeholder engagement with strong contribution to issue and risk management activities.

****Informations supplémentaires****

* As it is a Project Based Temporary role of 6 months, no benefits are provided.

**TotalEnergies valorise la diversité, promeut le développement individuel et offre des opportunités d'emploi égales à tous les candidats.**

data.location: Global Village, Dubai, United Arab Emirates
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Summary .**
The Senior Project Manager- Learning Experience on the Telehealth team oversees all aspects of online course development for medical professionals. Responsibilities include prioritizing new courses, coordinating subject-matter experts, and managing course creation, updates, and tra
data.jd_text:
**Job Summary .**
The Senior Project Manager- Learning Experience on the Telehealth team oversees all aspects of online course development for medical professionals. Responsibilities include prioritizing new courses, coordinating subject-matter experts, and managing course creation, updates, and translations.
  
  
**LOCATION**
Global remote
  
  
**REPORTING & WORKING RELATIONSHIPS**
The Senior Project Manager- Learning Experience reports to the Associate Director, Program Technology. Works closely with an in-house team of clinical training specialists and course developers, along with contracted subject-matter experts.
  
  
**Essential Job Functions / Key Areas Of Responsibility**
**Course Planning & Governance**

* Lead and maintain a structured intake and prioritization process for new course concepts, working closely with diverse stakeholders to balance donor priorities, Orbis program needs, and the evolving needs of Cybersight learners.
* Facilitate requirements gathering and alignment on goals across stakeholders.
* Establish project governance frameworks including documentation standards and QA checkpoints.
* Establish a systematic audit process with clinical experts and instructional designers to review existing courses and plan updates based on new evidence, evolving techniques, and learner needs.

**Project Management**

* Manage end-to-end projects for approximately 5 new online courses per year and for course localization initiatives.
* Create detailed project schedules, risk logs, and communication plans; mitigate risks and maintain momentum.
* Coordinate cross-functional contributors including SMEs, content creators, designers, translators, and vendors, including managing contracts and payments.
* Serve as the central communication point for all course development activities.
* Manage project budgets and track spending.

**Learning Experience & Content Development**

* Partner with instructional designers and clinical experts to shape course objectives and structure.
* Ensure all learning content meets quality, accessibility, and relevance standards for global LMIC contexts.
* Support development and/or sourcing of multimedia components (images, illustrations, video, assessments).

**Technical Coordination & LMS Integration**

* Coordinate with course developers to test, upload, and configure courses in the LMS.
* Validate course usability across devices and connectivity conditions.
* Collaborate on process improvements and relevant platform enhancements.
* (NOTE: Role requires familiarity with e-learning environments, but does not require deep technical work such as SCORM/xAPI authoring.)

**Launch, Evaluation & Continuous Improvement**

* Oversee coordinated launch plans including communications and stakeholder alignment.
* Monitor early performance, troubleshoot issues, and guide post-launch updates.
* Use learner analytics and feedback to refine development processes and inform future strategy.

**Cross-Team Collaboration & Capacity Building**

* Lead and model best-practice project management approaches for Orbis staff involved in course development, ensuring teams adopt effective workflows and high standards.
* Contribute to organizational learning around digital education trends and tools.
* Support hybrid or fully online Orbis training programs as needed.

**Qualifications & Experience**

* Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in project management, instructional design, education technology, public health, or related fields.
* Minimum 5 years of experience managing digital content or educational media projects.
* Experience in online learning, instructional design, or medical/clinical education preferred.
* Demonstrated experience leading cross-functional teams in a remote setting.
* Familiarity with LMS platforms and digital course development processes.
* Project management certification (PMP, PRINCE2, Scrum) desirable.

**Skills & Abilities**

* Strong organizational and planning skills; proven ability to manage multiple projects and wrangle stakeholders.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills across cultures and disciplines.
* Proactive problem-solving and risk mitigation skills.
* Ability to work independently and exercise sound judgment.
* Enthusiasm for technology-enabled learning, global health, and “technology for good.”
* Fluency in additional languages is a plus.
* Ability to travel internationally 1–2 times per year.

To learn more about Orbis, go to http://www.orbis.org
  
  
Orbis is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
  
  
As a global organization, we welcome qualified applicants from diverse backgrounds, cultures, and marginalized communities who are underrepresented in the international NGO sector who reflect the five Orbis values of Accountability, Commitment, Innovation, Integrity and Equity.

data.title: Director of Digital Asset Trading & Exchange Operations
data.raw_snippet:
**Role Overview:**

We are seeking a senior crypto trading leader to build, lead, and scale a new chapter in our digital asset trading capabilities. This is a strategic and hands-on role responsible for shaping and advancing the firm’s crypto trading, liquidity, exchange infrastructure, and derivati
data.jd_text:
**Role Overview:**

We are seeking a senior crypto trading leader to build, lead, and scale a new chapter in our digital asset trading capabilities. This is a strategic and hands-on role responsible for shaping and advancing the firm’s crypto trading, liquidity, exchange infrastructure, and derivatives capabilities. The role spans
**OTC, perpetuals, funding logic, market making, liquidity sourcing, hedging, and market structure design**
, while also playing an important part in the strategy surrounding token-related initiatives, exchange engagement, and broader platform growth.

The ideal candidate will bring a strong combination of
**trading leadership, derivatives expertise, liquidity and market-making experience, token exposure, and operational depth**
. They should understand how to build and scale a crypto trading function that is commercially effective, technically sound, and aligned with a more structured and institutional operating environment.

**Key Responsibilities:**

**Trading Leadership & Strategy**

* Lead and scale the crypto trading function across Spot, Derivatives, OTC, and Market Making.
* Define and execute the overall trading strategy in alignment with the company’s growth plans, platform roadmap, and commercial objectives.
* Take ownership of the trading desk’s performance, operating model, and long-term capability buildout.
* Develop a clear framework for how the business prices risk, sources liquidity, manages execution, and supports product growth.

**Derivatives, OTC & Pricing**

* Lead the design and launch of the derivatives trading capability from the ground up, including perpetual products, funding logic, hedging frameworks, and exposure management — this is a greenfield build requiring someone who has stood up a derivatives desk before.
* Lead the development of OTC pricing and execution frameworks, including liquidity sourcing, cross-venue hedging logic, and real-time pricing across crypto and fiat pairs.
* Ensure pricing, execution, and risk systems reflect actual market behaviour and support sustainable commercial performance.
* Improve the desk’s ability to manage complex market conditions, funding dynamics, and non-linear risk.

**Market Making, Liquidity & Market Structure**

* Design and oversee
  **market-making strategies**
  to optimise liquidity, spreads, order book depth, and execution efficiency.
* Define and execute
  **liquidity strategy across exchanges and providers**
  , improving access, reducing slippage, and enhancing execution quality.
* Shape
  **market structure, execution workflows, and venue strategy**
  to support client flow and platform growth.
* Develop frameworks to manage
  **flow dynamics, adverse selection, and client behaviour**
  effectively.
* Manage liquidity across
  **CEX and DEX environments (including AMMs)**
  , optimising execution in both off-chain and on-chain markets.
* Oversee integration with
  **custodial providers (e.g. Fireblocks)**
  , addressing latency, settlement, and operational coordination.
* Drive
  **end-to-end latency optimisation**
  across order routing, signing, and on-chain settlement.
* Maintain
  **tight spreads and consistent market depth across venues**
  , managing fragmented liquidity and multi-exchange listings.

**Token Strategy & Exchange Engagement**

* Lead token trading and liquidity strategy, including managing simultaneous listings across multiple Tier 1 CEXs, coordinating with exchange listing teams, meeting liquidity requirements, and managing launch windows and post-listing market support.
* Manage cross-exchange price consistency, arbitrage mitigation, and unified order book strategy to minimise price discrepancies and adverse arbitrage pressure when the token is live on multiple venues.
* Work closely with relevant stakeholders on exchange relationships, listing processes, and ongoing liquidity support across key venues.
* Ensure token and exchange strategy are underpinned by strong compliance awareness, including wash trading monitoring, market manipulation prevention, and adherence to exchange-specific listing rules and regulatory requirements.

**Spot Platform & Exchange Infrastructure**

* Oversee spot trading pair selection, curation, and lifecycle management on the platform, ensuring pairs are commercially viable and supported by adequate liquidity.
* Bring hands-on experience with
  **matching engine architecture, order book design, and trade settlement workflows**
  , working with technology teams to deliver best-in-class execution and liquidity on the platform.
* Optimise on-platform settlement processes and ensure seamless integration between the matching engine, custodial layer, and external liquidity sources.

**Risk, Governance & Infrastructure**

* Own risk management across trading books, liquidity operations, token-related exposure, and counterparty activity.
* Build robust controls around inventory, hedging, funding, execution, liquidity, and operational escalation.
* Partner with technology teams on execution tools, APIs, pricing systems, automation, and trading infrastructure.
* Ensure that trading activity operates within a strong governance and compliance framework suitable for a serious and growing platform.

**Cross-functional Leadership**

* Work closely with Product, Technology, Risk, Compliance, Operations, and senior management to align trading strategy with business objectives.
* Act as the senior internal owner for all matters related to trading logic, liquidity architecture, market structure, and execution design.
* Support decisions that sit across commercial strategy and trading reality, ensuring the platform is built in a way that is both scalable and market-relevant.

**Reporting & Senior Management Communication**

* Establish and maintain
  **structured reporting frameworks**
  covering trading performance, P&L, risk exposure, liquidity metrics, and market conditions, ensuring senior management has full visibility at all times.
* Deliver regular dashboards, commentary, and strategic updates to the executive team, translating complex trading dynamics into clear, actionable insights.
* Proactively escalate material risks, market events, or operational issues with clear recommendations and proposed action plans.

**Team Building & Management**

* Build, lead, and mentor a high-calibre trading team and supporting trading operations capability.
* Create a culture of accountability, speed, analytical rigour, and disciplined execution.
* Strengthen the function in a way that supports scale without compromising control, performance, or market edge.'

**Candidate Profile:**

* 7+ years of experience in crypto trading, derivatives, OTC, liquidity management, or market making.
* Proven track record of building or leading a crypto trading function within a structured or institutional environment.
* Strong expertise in derivatives trading, including perpetuals, funding, hedging, pricing, and risk management.
* Solid experience in OTC execution, liquidity sourcing, market making, and cross-venue trading strategies.
* Deep understanding of market structure, liquidity dynamics, exchange microstructure, and client flow behaviour.
* Experience working with tier-1 crypto exchanges, liquidity providers, and token-related trading environments (including listings or post-launch support).
* Good command of quantitative or systematic trading concepts, with the ability to collaborate with technical teams.
* Hands-on experience with DEXs (e.g. Uniswap), custodial platforms (e.g. Fireblocks), and hybrid CEX/DEX environments, including latency and settlement considerations.
* Familiarity with trading infrastructure, including matching engines, order books, and execution systems.
* Proven ability to manage fragmented liquidity, cross-exchange pricing, and arbitrage dynamics.
* Strong leadership, judgment, and crisis management skills in high-pressure market conditions.

**Preferred Background:**

* Experience within a crypto exchange, market maker, liquidity provider, prop trading firm, or institutional digital asset platform.
* Exposure to both centralised and decentralised trading environments.
* Experience supporting token listings, launches, or liquidity programmes.
* Familiarity with trading infrastructure, including pricing systems, funding mechanisms, and execution frameworks.
* Experience operating in institutional or regulated environments with strong governance and controls.
* Proven ability to manage external partners and counterparties, including exchanges, custodians, and liquidity providers.
* Understanding of exchange requirements, market-making obligations, and multi-jurisdictional considerations.

The incumbent is responsib
data.jd_text:
POSITION OBJECTIVE

The Senior Executive Director – Business Development provides enterprise-level strategic leadership to architect and execute at the forefront of institutional growth, sector expansion, and enterprise revenue performance aiming for 5X growth in 5 Years.

The incumbent is responsible for governing enterprise commercial architecture, sector diversification, institutional revenue sustainability, bid governance oversight, solution design alignment, pricing discipline, and commercial risk mitigation. This role ensures scalable, diversified, and margin-accretive growth aligned with long-term strategic objectives and institutional capability.

This role involves cultivating strong institutional and government relationships, identifying new market opportunities, and developing comprehensive growth strategies to expand reach across sectors and geographies. The Executive Director will lead the Business Development function to ensure alignment with overall mission and long-term strategic objectives.

ACADEMIC QUALIFICATIONS

A Master’s degree in Business Administration, Strategy, Management, or a related field is required with executive education or advanced commercial leadership qualifications being highly preferred.

Doctoral qualification considered advantageous

SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE

Experience

• Minimum of 15–20 years of progressive experience in Business Development, Commercial Strategy, or Enterprise Growth leadership.

• Proven track record managing diversified large-scale revenue portfolios with multi-sector expansion.

• Experience supervising Executive Director-level leaders and commercial vertical heads.

• Extensive experience in UAE institutional and government markets within multi-cultural environments.

Technical & Strategic Expertise

• Enterprise growth strategy execution aligned to ≥30% CAGR performance expectations.

• Revenue diversification and concentration risk governance frameworks.

• Pricing architecture, margin protection, and contract structuring expertise.

• Pipeline forecasting governance with structured variance control discipline.

• Thorough Knowledge of strategic revenue planning, enterprise forecasting, and commercial governance frameworks in post-secondary/tertiary education and lifelong learning.

• Knowledge of contract administration, negotiations, business planning and change management

• Ability to analyze and interpret revenue performance, pipeline data, and margin metrics.

• Excellent interpersonal and communication skills and a polished, professional presence with proven ability to build strong client relationships from the C-level of target clients.

• Exceptional presentation and bespoke solutions selling skills with proven success in with contemporary higher educational practices, learning theories, active learning and formative, authentic assessment products.

• Knowledge of communication, marketing, and public relation techniques aligned to growth strategyManagement & Leadership Skills

• Enterprise growth strategy execution aligned to ≥30% CAGR performance expectations.

• Revenue diversification and concentration risk governance frameworks.

• Pricing architecture, margin protection, and contract structuring expertise.

• Pipeline forecasting governance with structured variance control discipline

• Ability to build and motivate high-performance Business Development teams.

• Strong leadership capability in managing enterprise-level growth targets and transformation initiatives.

• Previous leadership experience within rapid change or transformation in Education & Training

• Superior management and leadership skills as demonstrated by the ability to form professional relationships at all levels of government, the organization and with appropriate external stakeholders as well as the ability to work effectively with a wide range of constituencies in a diverse community

data.raw_snippet:

* Act as the second line Subject Matter Expert for Digital
data.jd_text:
**Job Summary**
This role could be based in UAE and Hong Kong. When you start the application process you will be presented with a drop down menu showing all countries, Please ensure that you select a country where the role is based.
  
  

* Act as the second line Subject Matter Expert for Digital Assets (DA) as an asset class including advising across all relevant Risk Types (reputational risk, traded/market, treasury/capital and liquidity, operational, technology, cyber security, and credit risk).
* Lead provision of DA risk specific expertise to continuously enhance and maintain all elements of the DA Risk Management Approach (DARMA) which sets out the approach to risk management and the control framework within which risks are managed.
* Lead assessment of material risk types that the Group may be potentially exposed to arising from direct or indirect DA activities and direct Risk Framework Ownership to such risk types.
* Lead effectiveness reviews to assess relevant first and second line understand and accept their DA risk management responsibilities.
* Review and challenge that policies and standards are being adapted or created to address DA risks and training is available to staff (on a targeted basis).
* Review and challenge the assessment of DA Risk considerations in the Group’s Risk Appetite framework.
* Monitor that the Risk Framework Owners (RFOs) propose adequate risk appetite with DA risks evaluation for consideration and approval by the Board.
* Monitor Risk Type compliance to Board approved risk appetite with DA considerations using the risk information report covering all Risk Types; and highlight significant matters to the Group Chief Risk Officer (“GCRO”), senior management and senior risk committees.
* Review infrastructure to support DA reviews, and the monitoring of exposures and actions as required.
* Lead provision of regulatory interpretation for regulations classified as “Large-Scale” with Framework implications. This also includes working with countries to support their regulatory requirements.

**Key Responsibilities**
**Strategy**

* Influence the Group’s DA Strategy and activities, advising on alignment with specific Risk Type framework, policies, standards, risk registers and risk appetite.
* Actively engage thought leaders in the DA industries to improve risk understanding, innovation, exploration, and adoption of alternative risk models and data sources.
* Conduct regulatory interpretation and associated assessments for DA related initiatives with adherence to legal and regulatory requirements.
* Evaluate and advise on risks associated with DA projects, products and investments, including the assessment of blockchain technologies and tokenomics, to ensure robust risk mitigation strategies are in place.

**Business**

* Inform the strategic intent and collective agenda for the DA Risk Management Approach
* Influence stakeholders and DA Risk subject matter experts on thinking and approach for Digital Asset activities, including situations where the Group seeks to engage clients heavily exposed to DAs
* Advise Business on alignment of individual DA activities to the Group’s DA business strategy and execution within the risk framework and parameters.
* Direct the business to undertake appropriate evaluations with regards to DA stress scenarios with support from the Group Treasury and Group Stress Testing teams as required.
* Provide timely advice, support and challenge to enable compliance with all relevant laws and regulations and support pro-active and pre-emptive risk mitigation.

**Processes**

* The Candidate is responsible for discharging Policy Owner related responsibilities as defined in the ERMF:
* Ensuring that processes within the DA Risk Management central function are identified and defined by Process Owners, and ensuring the completeness and accuracy of these identified processes; and
* Reviewing, challenging, and providing oversight of the DA risk profile monitored bythe DA Risk Management Function process universe.

**People & Talent**

* Create a culture of continuous learning and development
* Foster an open environment to stimulate creativity and develop innovative solution to supports Group’s aspiration.
* Lead through example and build the appropriate culture and values.
* Set appropriate tone and expectations from the team and work in collaboration with risk and control partners.

**Risk Management**
**For DAs Initiatives, Lead Inter-alia**

* Oversight of development of the risk assessment approach (DARMA) for any planned business direct exposure to DAs and/or entities heavily exposed to DAs.
* Required DA risk reviews:
* Provide subject matter expertise on DA risks and their interactions to core banking Business risks (across CIB/WRB) including capital and liquidity impacts, and traded and credit risk limits
* Review and challenge enhanced DA Due Diligence reviews on Business DA clients, products, initiatives and investments to enable adherence to DARMA.
* Evaluate DA risk assessments for confirmation by existing Business authorities including evaluation of blockchains and tokens, DA key management and wallet management infrastructure, operations and partnerships with DA third parties e.g. DA custodians, exchanges, staking providers etc
* Direct and review notification requirements to relevant regulators
* Oversight and challenge for the Group’s DA Risk Profile and activities including development and implementation of appropriate infrastructure to support DA reviews, and the monitoring of exposures and actions as required.
* Direct DA considerations in Risk Appetite by relevant Risk Framework Owners.
* monitoring the approved metrics through the risk information reports on an ongoing basis and that incidents of breach are being escalated by the relevant Business and/or Risk Type to the respective Committees.

**Governance**

* Run and maintain a governance forum/committee structure to provide effective risk oversight of the DA Risk Profile across the Group.
* Conduct annual effectiveness reviews to identify improvement areas and implement necessary actions.
* Lead review and challenge of on-going compliance with and effectiveness of the risk control environment and overall exposure and risk levels to DAs
* Monitor top and emerging risks through forward looking indicators, taking into consideration to external events which may be material.
* Review and assess management action being taken to mitigate their impact.

**Regulatory & Business Conduct**

* Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct.
* Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct.
* Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters.

**Key stakeholders**
Internal
  
  
Digital Assets
  
  

* Members of the Group Management Team
* Digital Assets Centre of Excellence
* Business heads
* Group and Country Risk Framework Owners
* Digital Assets SMEs
* Group Head, Internal Audit

External
  
  

* Home and host regulators including Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) and Financial Conduct Authority (FCA)
* The Group's external auditors

**Skills And Experience**

* Traded Risk
* Digital Assets
* Credit
* Traded Risk
* Operational and Cyber Security
* Reputational
* Compliance
* Treasury

**Qualifications**

* Strong academic background
* >15 years of experience in banking / financial services specifically capital, liquidity risk, traded and credit risk.
* >15 years of experience in emerging Digital technology
* >5 years direct experience in Digital Asset technology working for a Digital Asset Service Provider
* Deep expertise (at least 5 years) of evaluating financial and non-financial risks associated with blockchains, tokens, CBDCs, stablecoins and cryptocurrencies.
* A clear understanding of novel risks in DA and how these risks manifest within relevant Risk Types such as Reputational, Traded/Market, Treasury/Capital and Liquidity, Operational, Technology and Cyber Security Risks, and Credit Risk.
* Broad experience across diverse business models within banking and Fintechs
* Strong expertise within risk management including governance and frameworks including proven verbal and written communication skills to the Board level.
* Thought leadership in digital-related risks (Digital-asset risk, technology risk, ICS risk, resilience)
* Strong understanding of Digital-assets and fintech industry trends / developments, risk implications and impacts on the financial sector
* Knowledge of key regulatory and supervisory frameworks. Ideal - direct experience of working closely with supervisors / regulators
* Strong stakeholder management, communication and influencing skills
* Excellent interpersonal skills, multicultural awareness and sensitivity
* Programme/project management
* Experience paper writing for senior level committees.
* Creativity and appetite for continuous improvement

**About Standard Chartered**
We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us.
  
  
Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion.
  
  
**Together We**

* Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do
* Never settle, continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well
* Are better together, we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term

**What We Offer**
**In line with our Fair Pay Charter,**
we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing.
  
  

* Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance, with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations.
* Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum.
* Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns.
* Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits
* A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning.
* Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation, one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential.

data.title: Director of Project Execution
data.raw_snippet:
**Together we make the difference**
We are a leading provider of innovative and high-performance solutions for airport logistics. Our portfolio includes products and solutions for baggage and cargo handling, high-end software for the digitalization of logistics processes as well as an extensive rang
data.jd_text:
**Together we make the difference**
We are a leading provider of innovative and high-performance solutions for airport logistics. Our portfolio includes products and solutions for baggage and cargo handling, high-end software for the digitalization of logistics processes as well as an extensive range of maintenance and services. Major customers include renowned airports and airlines around the globe.
  
  
We are looking for an experienced
**Director of Project Execution**
who will
**report directly to the Executive Management**
and oversee multiple airport BHS and MHS projects. You will provide guidance and leadership to the Project Execution and Site Management teams, foster collaboration across internal functions and external stakeholders, and ensure projects are delivered safely, on-time, on-budget, contract compliant, and to the required quality through to handover.
  
  
**What are my responsibilities?**

* Lead end-to-end delivery of airport BHS/MHS programs, ensuring safe, on-budget, contract-compliant execution.
* Serve as senior client and stakeholder lead, aligning airport authorities/operators, consultants/PMCs, contractors, and internal teams.
* Own the full lifecycle from engineering coordination through installation, integration, testing, commissioning, ORAT, and handover to Service.
* Drive strong project governance (steerco, reviews, workshops), ensuring clear decisions, actions, and follow-up.
* Manage portfolio performance across multi-site/multi-country matrix teams; control KPIs, risk, change, and escalations, including recovery/turnaround.
* Ensure effective site execution in live airport environments, including M&E installation, upgrades, brownfield works, and system interface management.
* Ensure completeness and quality of technical/contract documentation and reporting across the project lifecycle.
* Lead successful handovers to Life-Cycle Services, ensuring continuity, training, spares, warranty transition, and sign-off.
* Support Sales & Bid activities to develop opportunities and ensure executable delivery strategies and resourcing inputs.
* Build and lead high-performing teams through coaching, accountability, and continuous improvement.

**What do I need to qualify for the role?**

* 10+ years delivering complex engineering/automation/site projects; strong ownership of cost, schedule, quality, and safety.
* Proven leadership in multi-stakeholder environments with strong client-facing and executive communication capability.
* Strong experience in airport BHS or MHS systems including site execution and commissioning; ORAT exposure is a plus.
* Solid understanding of automation integration environments (mechanical/electrical, PLC/SCADA/controls, interfaces).
* Strong commercial and contractual discipline: change control, risk/opportunity management, claims awareness.
* Proficient in project tools (e.g., MS Project/Primavera, MS Office; Jira/Confluence is a plus).
* Willingness to travel and operate in dynamic airport environments.
* Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.

data.title: Project Director-Ccgt
data.location: Al Taweelah, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
data.raw_snippet:
**Project Overview**

The Taweelah C Project is a landmark greenfield development in the Emirate of Abu Dhabi, representing one of the region's most advanced and efficient combined cycle gas turbine (CCGT) power plants. With an installed capacity of approximately 2.6 GW, the project will play a crit
data.jd_text:
**Project Overview**

The Taweelah C Project is a landmark greenfield development in the Emirate of Abu Dhabi, representing one of the region's most advanced and efficient combined cycle gas turbine (CCGT) power plants. With an installed capacity of approximately 2.6 GW, the project will play a critical role in supporting the UAE's growing electricity demand while delivering high efficiency and lower emissions through state-of-the-art technology.

The project brings together leading international partners with Al Jomaih Energy and Water Co. Ltd. (AEW) and Sembcorp Utilities Pte. Ltd. (Sembcorp) together with TAQA acting as the Project Company

Taweelah C offers a unique opportunity to be part of a high-profile infrastructure project that will set new benchmarks in performance, reliability, and sustainability in the region's power sector.

**Location:**
Taweelah, Abu Dhabi, UAE (Site is 30 to 40 Kms from Abu Dhabi city)

**Duration:**
2.5 years

**Job Overview**

To lead and oversee all construction activities for the Taweelah C Power Project. Ensuring the timely, on-budget, and quality delivery of the asset, meeting contractual requirements, and design specifications for reliable and sustainable operation over the Power Purchase Agreement period.

Implementing the HSSE program and supervision in the Project Company and Owner's team, fostering a culture of health, safety, security, and environment in alignment with Project Company and Stakeholder targets.

Monitoring, coordinating, and reporting on multiple Contractors' activities at the site, challenging and negotiating with the EPC contractor when necessary to protect the Project Company's interests.

Leading a highly skilled team and directing the Owner's Engineer to ensure seamless construction with highest levels of HSSE within the necessary quality, time and budget.

Facilitating seamless communication and information exchanges among stakeholders, including project partners, local communities, and external parties.

**Requirements**

**Your Job Functions (Duties & Responsibilities)**

* Oversee plant construction to ensure compliance with quality, schedule, and budget agreements.
* Manage HSSE implementation onsite and offsite, maintaining a strong HSSE culture.
* Coordinate activities of the Owner's Engineer, local contractors, local authorities and interface partners and Contractors.
* Monitor and enforce EPC Contractor's compliance with EPC Contract, Owner's requirements and International Standards
* Act as the primary link between the off-taker, Owner's Engineer, EPC Contractor, third party, the Operator, --etc to align battery limits and tie-ins.
* Align technical and schedule interfaces between different contractors, ensuring contract conformity and project needs.
* Monitor Engineering & Procurement, QA/QC and OE ensuring the EPC Contractor issuing the engineering packages and deliver procurement items ahead of construction needs.
* Organize and lead construction and progress management meetings with all stakeholders.
* Identify, assess, and escalate technical deviations, construction risks, and non-conformities to the Project Company EMD, proposing effective mitigation and recovery measures.
* Manage contractual, commercial, and technical matters arising under the EPC Contract and WTA, in coordination with legal and commercial teams.
* Review, evaluate, and provide recommendations on change orders, variations, and claims submitted by contractors and service providers.
* Lead resolution meetings for technical and construction issues encountered on-site and off-site during execution.
* Lead QA/QC oversight and manage the SPV engineering teams including the Owner's Engineer  to witness, verify, and document key inspections and tests in accordance with the approved Inspection and Test Plans (ITPs) and project requirements.
* Lead risk assessment and risk identification workshops with the EPC Contractor and Owner's Engineer, ensuring all project risks are identified, formally recorded in the risk register periodically, and supported by clear mitigation and action plans.
* Interact with Sponsors, Lender's Engineer, Insurance Advisers, and O&M teams.
* Issue progress reports and technical memoranda as required.
* Supervise milestone completion certificates, invoices, and mechanical completion certificates.
* Lead/participate as required in technical review committees as they may be defined during the construction period with sponsors.
* Facilitate the handover of the Project to the O&M Team.
* Host visitors at the site and manage communications with stakeholders.

**Contractual/Commercial**

* Timely reporting of contractual/commercial variances and proposing mitigation or alternative solutions.
* Assist in writing contractual letters to stakeholders.
* Identify and anticipate deliverables to relevant parties.
* Coordinate progress of commissioning and testing with Off-taker and Dispatch Control Centre.
* Assist in claims management with Contractors and Service Providers.

**Qualifications & Skill Sets**

* University Degree in Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, or Chemical Engineering.
* Additional qualifications (project management/Construction Management) are preferred
* 15 +years of project and site experience in large scale power projects, some in CCGT projects.
* Extensive knowledge of utilities and construction of Combined Cycle Power Plants.
* Experience in large infrastructure project execution.
* Experience with Turnkey EPC contractors. (with Chinese EPC contractor preferred)
* Strong technical knowledge and understanding of construction processes, contracts, and international standards.
* Contract/claim management and negotiation skills.
* Familiarity with BOO project structures, project finance documents, EPC contracts, PPAs.
* Excellent communication and reporting skills.
* Ability to work independently and lead teams.
* Strong ethical standards.
