data.jd_text:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!
  
  
At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an innovative culture that empowers you to achieve your full potential. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible.
  
  
**Job Description**
Parsons is looking for a highly experienced
**Senior Construction Director**
to join their team! The
**Senior Construction Director**
will play a pivotal leadership role in the delivery of one of the world’s largest and most complex urban developments. Operating at true mega-project scale, this role is responsible for overseeing the construction of major infrastructure networks, high-quality public realm, and iconic Tier 1 architectural assets.
  
  
The position requires strategic oversight of multiple PMCM consultants and contractors, ensuring alignment with the client’s vision, program objectives, and world-class quality expectations. The role will act as a key interface between the client, delivery partners, and senior stakeholders, driving performance across technical, commercial, and operational functions to ensure successful project execution.
  
  
**What You'll Be Doing;**

* Provide executive-level leadership for construction delivery across multiple zones/packages within the program.
* Oversee the performance of PMCM consultants and contractors, ensuring adherence to schedule, budget, quality, and safety objectives.
* Drive integrated delivery across infrastructure, public realm, and complex building assets.
* Ensure alignment of construction activities with the approved masterplan, phasing strategy, and operational requirements.
* Act as a primary point of contact for the client on all construction-related matters.
* Lead high-level coordination with client representatives, government authorities, and key stakeholders.
* Provide clear, accurate, and timely reporting on project status, risks, and mitigation strategies.
* Support executive decision-making through strategic advice and data-driven insights.
* Manage and direct PMCM consultants, ensuring effective delivery of construction management services.
* Challenge and guide PMCMs on technical, sequencing, constructability, and risk-related matters.
* Ensure consistency in standards, processes, and reporting across all delivery partners.
* Lead resolution of complex technical and coordination issues across multiple disciplines.
* Monitor and drive progress against program milestones, proactively addressing delays and constraints.
* Identify and manage key project risks, implementing mitigation and recovery strategies where required.
* Oversee construction sequencing and logistics planning at a program-wide level.
* Support commercial teams in managing contractor performance, variations, and claims.
* Ensure construction delivery aligns with contractual obligations and procurement strategies.
* Contribute to dispute avoidance and resolution strategies.
* Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing, multidisciplinary construction team.
* Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, and excellence.
* Ensure appropriate resource planning and capability within the team.
* Champion best-in-class HSE standards and ensure compliance across all works.
* Drive quality assurance and control processes to deliver world-class assets.
* Support sustainability objectives in line with project and national goals.

**What Required Skills You'll Bring;**

* Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field (Master’s degree preferred).
* Minimum of 20+ years of experience in construction delivery, with at least 10 years in senior leadership roles on large-scale or mega projects.
* Proven track record in delivering complex infrastructure, public realm, and high-profile architectural developments (Tier 1 or landmark assets).
* Extensive experience managing PMCM consultants and Tier 1 contractors in a client-facing role.
* Strong knowledge of international construction standards, contract forms (e.g., FIDIC), and delivery models.
* Demonstrated ability to manage large, multidisciplinary teams and multiple stakeholders.
* Experience in the Middle East, particularly in KSA giga-projects, is highly desirable.
* Professional certifications (e.g., PMP, MRICS, PE) are advantageous.

Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels no matter the race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, disability or genetic information.
  
  
We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!
  
  
Parsons is aware of fraudulent recruitment practices. To learn more about recruitment fraud and how to report it, please refer to https://www.parsons.com/fraudulent-recruitment/.

data.jd_text:
Mace combines construction expertise with consultancy to unlock potential in every person or project and redefine the boundaries of ambition. Our values shape the way we consult and define the people we want to join us on our journey.
  
  
**The Project**
This is an opportunity to work on one of the largest programmes in Saudi Arabia. This unique project consists of major mixed-use development including sports facilities, entertainment, and arts along with all associated infrastructure.
  
  
As associate construction director, you will serve as the client’s representative on-site, leading the day-to-day delivery of major civil and infrastructure works. You will manage and supervise main contractors to ensure full technical compliance, high-quality execution, and strict program adherence while driving a best-in-class health and safety culture. Your role will be pivotal in safeguarding client objectives, resolving technical issues, maintaining effective interface coordination, and ensuring the successful delivery of complex infrastructure assets.
  
  
**You'll Be Responsible For**

* Lead safe, high-quality, compliant delivery while championing well-being, inclusivity, and strong health and safety standards.
* Build trusted client and stakeholder relationships, translating strategic vision into clear delivery plans.
* Uphold ethical, fiscally responsible project governance and embed long-term strategic value.
* Empower teams through guidance, knowledge sharing, and best‑practice delivery excellence.
* Manage a strong health, safety, and well-being culture, ensuring compliance, rigorous audits, and effective critical controls.
* Build trusted client and stakeholder relationships, translating strategic vision into clear delivery plans.
* Oversee multidisciplinary coordination, stakeholder engagement, and district-wide interfaces to drive safe, high‑quality delivery.
* Ensure adherence to execution plans, standards, and documentation while resolving design or contract gaps.
* Provide clear reporting, lead project meetings, and champion continuous improvement across delivery activities.
* Lead day-to-day site operations, ensuring safe systems of work, effective method statements, strong permit-to-work controls, and robust supervision of contractors.
* Monitor on-site progress, identify issues early, and implement corrective actions to maintain programme, quality, and safety benchmarks.
* Oversee multidisciplinary coordination, stakeholder engagement, and district-wide interfaces to ensure seamless, high-quality delivery.
* Ensure strict adherence to execution plans, technical standards, inspection and test plans (ITPs), and project documentation, proactively resolving design or contract gaps.

**You’ll Need To Have**

* Degree qualified with professional membership (MCIOB, MAPM, MRICS, MICE) and strong technical, commercial, and sustainability awareness.
* Experience delivering complex, large-scale infrastructure projects, including roads, highways, utilities, and major civil works.
* Demonstrated leadership of multidisciplinary delivery teams, stakeholder engagement, and robust project governance.
* Excellent communication skills with proficiency in enterprise-level project management and reporting systems.
* Necessary visa criteria for the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and have relevant work experience working on high-valued projects/programmes for a project management consultancy business, which are required for immigration and client approval.

**Our values**
**Safety first - Going home safe and well:**
We champion a safe, diverse, and inclusive working environment, understanding the importance of well-being in every team.
  
  
**Client focus - Deliver on our promise:**
We own the quality of deliverables and strategic outcomes and build long-term relationships with our clients.
  
  
**Integrity - Always do the right thing:**
We influence positive outcomes within our industry while always aligning with our compliance obligations.
  
  
**Create opportunity - For our people to excel:**
We champion a continuous improvement culture throughout all activities, inspiring our people and teams to develop.
  
  
Mace is an inclusive employer and welcomes interest from a diverse range of candidates. Even if you feel you do not fulfill all the criteria, please apply, as you may still be the best candidate for this role or another role within our organisation.
  
  
We are also open to discussing part-time, flexible, and hybrid working options if suitable within the role.

data.raw_snippet:
We are seeking an experienced
**Director - Residential FP&A**
to establish and lead the financial planning and analysis function for a newly established Residential subsidiary within a large-scale Saudi giga‑project.
  
  
This role will play a critical part in setting up the financial foundations o
data.jd_text:
We are seeking an experienced
**Director - Residential FP&A**
to establish and lead the financial planning and analysis function for a newly established Residential subsidiary within a large-scale Saudi giga‑project.
  
  
This role will play a critical part in setting up the financial foundations of a newly formed residential platform while operating within the governance, funding, and reporting architecture of an existing giga‑project.
  
  
The role will act as a key financial interface between the Residential Business Unit, Corporate Finance, and regulatory stakeholders.
  
  
The Director will be responsible for designing and embedding best-in-class financial planning, forecasting and reporting processes for a large-scale residential portfolio.
  
  
The role requires a hands-on leader with deep real estate/development experience, capable of building financial discipline, enabling informed decision-making, and ensuring strong financial governance across complex, multi-year projects.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**
**FP&A leadership**

* Lead the FP&A function for the Residential Business Unit and the newly established residential operating subsidiary
* Enhance and manage the budgeting, forecasting, and management reporting frameworks for residential development projects within a giga‑project environment
* Develop and embed financial governance frameworks, controls, and reporting standards aligned with Corporate Finance, Board, and investment committee requirements
* Ensure financial structures, controls, and reporting are fit for purpose for the residential business unit
* Act as the primary financial counterpart to Corporate Finance, Treasury, Internal Audit, and External Auditors for residential activities.

**Financial planning and portfolio oversight**

* Prepare and maintain consolidated financial forecasts for the residential portfolio, including capex planning and cash flow projections.
* Monitor financial performance across residential projects and provide variance analysis against approved business plans.
* Manage portfolio‑level cash flow planning, funding requirements, and liquidity forecasts, with specific focus on off‑plan sales and escrow funding dynamics
* Produce regular financial reports and dashboards for senior leadership and investment committees.

**Escrow Management, Audit, and Regulatory Compliance (REGA / WAFI)**

* Establish, oversee, and govern escrow account structures for off‑plan residential sales in full compliance with REGA and WAFI regulations.
* Coordinate with banks, escrow trustees, legal advisors, and Corporate Treasury on escrow account setup, usage rules, drawdown mechanisms, and reporting
* Ensure accurate tracking and reconciliation of escrow inflows, permitted outflows, and balances at project and portfolio level
* Lead the preparation of escrow-related reporting and submissions required by REGA and other relevant authorities
* Act as the primary finance lead for all internal and external audits related to escrow accounts, residential projects, and the residential subsidiary
* Ensure robust audit trails, documentation standards, and financial controls are in place to support regulatory reviews and statutory audits

**Business partnering with development teams**

* Partner closely with development, commercial, and sales teams to support financial decision‑making across the full residential development lifecycle
* Provide financial insights on development projects through the development stage gate lifecycle and support feasibility studies
* Align cashflow forecasts to residential projects progress and revised development programs

**Systems and process development**

* Lead the implementation of FP&A tools, financial models, and reporting systems to support residential portfolio and subsidiary monitoring
* Support integration with corporate finance systems, including SAP FICO, ensuring residential and escrow transactions are accurately reflected
* Contribute to the development of data visualization and performance dashboards (e.g., Power BI) for executive and regulatory reporting
* Drive continuous improvements in reporting efficiency, automation, data quality, and analytical depth.

**Leadership**

* Build, lead, and scale a high‑performing FP&A team as the residential platform grows.
* Establish a high‑accountability culture with strong technical rigor, commercial mindset, and compliance awareness.

**Requirements**

* Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field
* CPA or equivalent qualification is essential.
* 12-15+ years of experience in financial planning and analysis, development finance, or project finance within the real estate sector.
* Strong experience working on large-scale real estate developments, ideally with projects exceeding $ 1B in capex.
* Experience working with regional real estate developers in the Middle East is required.
* Proven track record of building financial processes and systems in a new or evolving organization.
* Strong financial modeling skills and advanced Excel proficiency.
* Experience with ERP systems such as SAP FICO; familiarity with Power BI or similar visualization tools is a plus.
* Exposure to feasibility modeling tools such as Estate Master is advantageous.
* Strong communication skills and ability to work effectively with senior stakeholders in an international environment

Responsible for monitoring and supervising assigned construction project(s) on a daily basis throughout various project phases, including coordination, progress review, quality control, work scheduling, outage requirements, safety compliance, testing and commissioning, and pro
data.jd_text:
**Position Summary:**

Responsible for monitoring and supervising assigned construction project(s) on a daily basis throughout various project phases, including coordination, progress review, quality control, work scheduling, outage requirements, safety compliance, testing and commissioning, and processing contractors’ invoices. The role requires solid experience in EHV/HV project management and construction activities related to Substations, Underground Cables, Overhead Transmission Lines, Civil, Electro-Mechanical, and Protection works.

**Duties and Responsibilities:**

* Monitor and supervise assigned construction project(s) on a daily basis throughout various project phases.
* Establish project and quality control procedures.
* Prepare and monitor work schedules.
* Identify outage requirements.
* Coordinate with contractors and SEC departments.
* Review project progress.
* Review design drawings, materials, and submittals.
* Ensure compliance with Safety and Loss Prevention requirements.
* Witness testing and commissioning activities.
* Process contractors’ invoices.

**Technical Knowledge:**

* Thorough knowledge of EHV/HV project management and construction activities related to Substations, Underground Cables, Overhead Transmission Lines, Civil, Electro-Mechanical, and Protection works.

**Minimum Qualifications and Experience:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Civil, or Mechanical Engineering.
* Minimum twelve (12) years of experience, including at least six (6) years as a
  **Site Manager**
  in a large power utility company in EHV/HV project management and construction activities related to Substations, Underground Cables, Overhead Transmission Lines, Civil, Electro-Mechanical, and Protection works.
* For Saudi nationals: minimum six (6) years of experience, including at least three (3) years as a Site Manager.
* Professional certification in project management or a related discipline is preferred.

data.title: Software Engineering Director
data.raw_snippet:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
  
  
Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together u
data.jd_text:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
  
  
Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together under a single director of engineering. Once you have passed the first round of interviews we will discuss specific software products which would be a good fit for your interests.
  
  
We believe that open source is just starting to transform the tech sector and enterprise compute. Our goal is to make open source easier, more reliable and more secure for deployment and development. We strive to be the provider of 'most software to most companies'. To deliver on that ambition, our engineers are carefully selected from the applicants across the globe. We select for brilliance and motivation to take open source to the next level. Our engineering directors set the pace and the direction to help teams achieve more than they realised they could, and feel proud of the result.
  
  
Even though this is a senior management position, managing managers, we expect Engineering Directors to be outstanding developers with the confidence to lead by example across the full range of activities in their teams. You need to understand what great code looks like in the language and genre of your product - whether that is a web service, or a system daemon, or a desktop application, or a distributed system. You'll need to know how to drive testing and benchmarking to improve the clarity in your teams around quality and performance. You'll also need to be confident to set and defend high expectations of work ethic, consistency, focus and learning.
  
  
We have director level roles across a wide range of engineering domains, including:
  
  

* Python and Golang
* C / C++ / Rust
* Data infrastructure
* HTML / CSS / JavaScript / Typescript / React
* Flutter
* Distro packaging and systems
* SAAS and web microservices
* Kernel
* Servers
* Graphics, Browser and Desktop
* Silicon enablement and embedded devices
* Product Security

If your domain of expertise isn't listed above, yet you feel it's relevant to Canonical, then feel free to apply anyway. We will route you to the most suitable team.
  
  
Location: The role is remote, with positions available in all time zones. It includes a requirement for global travel for twice per year, for trips up to two weeks long.
  
  
What you'll do
  
  

* Lead multiple teams of engineers, ranging from graduate to senior
* Develop your engineering managers and maintain Canonical culture
* Hire staff level engineers for specific roles like performance and quality
* Coach, mentor, and offer career development feedback
* Identify and measure indicators of team health and productivity
* Ensure rounded delivery including community participation and docs
* Implement disciplined engineering processes and defend them
* Set and measure progress against benchmark goals for speed and efficiency
* Represent your product to stakeholders, partners, and customers
* Develop and evangelise great engineering and organisational practices
* Plan and manage progress on agreed goals and projects
* Be an active part of the leadership team, collaborating with other leaders
* Collaborate with leads for related or dependent products

What we're looking for in you
  
  

* An exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
* Undergraduate degree in Computer Science or STEM, or a compelling narrative about your alternative path
* Extensive management experience and confidence to set high expectations
* Outstanding Linux based software engineering track record
* Drive and a track record of going above-and-beyond expectations
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
* Public speaking and presentation skills
* A love of developing and growing people and a track record of it
* Experience in leading, coaching and mentoring software developers
* Organised and able to ensure your team delivers timely, high quality results
* Well-organised, self-starting and able to deliver to schedule
* Professional manner interacting with colleagues, partners, and community
* You have advanced expertise in your own domain
* You are knowledgeable and passionate about software development
* You have solid experience working in an agile development environment
* You have a demonstrated drive for continual learning
* Builds trust, relationships and confidence
* Result-oriented, with a personal drive to meet commitments
* Ability to travel twice a year, for company events up to two weeks each

**Additional Skills We Value**

* Experience in a developer advocacy or community role
* Open source community engagement and leadership
* Ops and system administration experience
* Performance engineering and security experience

What we offer you
  
  
We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
  
  

* Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events

**About Canonical**
Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Canonical has been a remote-first company since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
  
  
Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
  
  
We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

data.title: Technical Director
data.raw_snippet:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!
  
  
At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an in
data.jd_text:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!
  
  
At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an innovative culture that empowers you to achieve your full potential. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible.
  
  
**Job Description**
Parsons is looking for an amazingly talented
**Technical Director**
to join our team in Riyadh! In this role you will get to lead and direct the technical aspects of a mega infrastructure project exceeding $2 Billion in total installed cost.
  
  
The Technical Director will be responsible for overseeing all technical aspects of the project, ensuring that engineering, design review and technical disciplines are properly coordinated and that the project is delivered to the highest standards of quality, safety, and efficiency.
  
  
**What You Will Be Doing**

* Provide technical leadership and direction to the project team.
* Ensure that all technical aspects of the project are aligned with the project goals and objectives.
* Oversee the design and engineering processes of all disciplines to ensure the design is produced in line with the requirements of the project.
* Coordinate with project managers, engineers, and other stakeholders to ensure seamless integration of technical solutions.
* Facilitate communication between different technical disciplines and project teams.
* Implement and maintain quality control procedures to ensure the highest standards of technical excellence.
* Conduct regular technical audits and reviews to identify and mitigate risks.
* Identify and resolve technical issues and challenges that arise during the project lifecycle.
* Provide innovative solutions to complex design and engineering problems.
* Manage technical resources, including personnel, equipment, and materials.
* Ensure that technical teams are adequately staffed and equipped to meet project demands.
* Engage with clients, regulatory bodies, and other stakeholders to ensure their technical requirements and expectations are met.
* Represent the project in technical meetings and presentations.
* Assist in the development and management of the project budget and schedule from a technical perspective.
* Ensure that technical activities are completed on time and within budget.

**What Required Skills You Will Bring**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Civil Engineering, or a related field.
* 20+ years of experience in design review, engineering and technical management, in large-scale multi-disciplinary infrastructure projects (railways, large main utilities, highways, bridges, airports).
* Strong leadership and team management skills.
* Ability to engage effectively with clients, appointed design consultants, regulatory bodies, and other stakeholders.
* Excellent problem-solving and decision-making abilities.
* Proficiency in engineering software and tools.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
* Proven ability to perform in a supervisory/managerial capacity, with excellent written and oral communication skills.
* Strong acumen in sustainable design, engineering and construction innovations.
* Experience in KSA and/or the GCC.
* Thorough knowledge of industry practices and regulations.
* Thorough knowledge of current technology and the capabilities and efficiencies of specific engineering software for use in completing engineering assignments.
* Proven track record of successful project delivery.
* Experience in conducting technical audits and reviews.
* Knowledge of budget and schedule management from a technical perspective.

**What Desired Skills You Will Bring**

* Professional Engineer registration with active membership in a professional engineering society

Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels no matter the race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, disability or genetic information.
  
  
We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!
  
  
Parsons is aware of fraudulent recruitment practices. To learn more about recruitment fraud and how to report it, please refer to https://www.parsons.com/fraudulent-recruitment/.

data.raw_snippet:
**About the Role:**

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Project Manager to support the delivery of mission-critical projects for public sector and large enterprise clients in Saudi Arabia. In this role, you will assist in managing Digital Infrastructure initiatives, including
data.jd_text:
**About the Role:**

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Project Manager to support the delivery of mission-critical projects for public sector and large enterprise clients in Saudi Arabia. In this role, you will assist in managing Digital Infrastructure initiatives, including Cybersecurity, Digital Transformation, Digital Networks, and Data Centre projects.

This is a client-facing position where you will be expected to demonstrate a proactive, client-first mindset, while contributing to high-quality project delivery, clear communication, and continuous improvement.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Support the end-to-end management of digital infrastructure projects, from planning and scoping to execution and delivery.
* Work closely with senior project managers and stakeholders to gather requirements and ensure alignment with business and regulatory needs.
* Maintain accurate project documentation, including schedules, reports, and risk logs.
* Assist in preparing client-facing dashboards and reports highlighting KPIs, timelines, and project status.
* Help build and maintain strong client relationships, supporting communication throughout the project lifecycle.
* Contribute to the preparation of status updates, progress reports, and presentations.
* Support alignment of projects with strategic initiatives such as Saudi Vision 2030.
* Track project timelines, budgets, and resources, ensuring tasks are completed on schedule.
* Assist in identifying project risks and support the development of mitigation plans.
* Work within Agile, Waterfall, or Hybrid project management methodologies as required.

**Qualifications & Skills:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology or a related field.
* Fluent in Arabic and English (spoken and written).
* Basic understanding of cybersecurity, networking, data centres, and digital transformation concepts.
* Strong organizational skills with high attention to detail.
* Good communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work in a team environment.
* Analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities.
* Eagerness to learn and grow within a fast-paced project environment.

If you are passionate about technology and eager to build a strong foundation in project management while shaping the digital future of Saudi Arabia, we welcome your application.

Send your CV to: careers@3mdata.tech

Dar Almasalla is a Saudi-owned marble brand with an ISO-certified manufacturing
data.jd_text:
**Job Title:**
Technical Office Manager – Marble & Stone Works
**Location:**
Riyadh, Saudi Arabia (On-site)
**Reporting to:**
General Manager / Operations Director
**Employment Type:**
Full-time

**About Dar Almasalla**

Dar Almasalla is a Saudi-owned marble brand with an ISO-certified manufacturing facility and three showrooms in Riyadh. We execute premium marble works across residential, commercial, and hospitality projects from custom sinks and vanities to full-scale architectural finishes. We operate at the intersection of craft and precision.

**Role Summary**
Own the technical office function end-to-end: drawings, shop drawing packages, cross-team coordination between factory and site, and execution follow-up all moving in parallel without dropping the ball.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Own technical office output: shop drawings, technical details, submittal packages, and revision cycles
* Review and interpret architectural drawings, specs, and contract documents for marble scope
* Coordinate with factory/production on material requirements, cut lists, and fabrication feasibility
* Liaise with site teams on installation sequencing, technical clarifications, and RFI management
* Track submittals, approvals, and revision logs; escalate blockers proactively
* Support quantity take-off and material scheduling for active projects
* Maintain documentation standards across all project files
* Flag technical and coordination risks before they become site problems

**Required Qualifications**

* Bachelor's in Architecture, Architectural Engineering, or Civil Engineering
* 5+ years in a technical office or project coordination role
* Marble, natural stone, or closely related sector experience (fit-out, façade, architectural finishes)
* Proficient in AutoCAD; working knowledge of MS Office/Project
* Able to read and mark up construction drawings independently
* Strong coordination instincts you close loops, you don't leave them open

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience interfacing with a production/factory environment, not just site
* Familiarity with Saudi construction project norms and consultant submission processes
* Arabic/English bilingual (both will be used daily)

**What Success Looks Like (30/60/90 Days)**

* 30 days: Fully onboarded on active projects; no technical submission lag
* 60 days: Running the technical office independently; factory-site coordination flowing without escalation
* 90 days: Identified and resolved at least one systemic coordination gap; contributing to process improvement

**Compensation & Benefits**
Competitive package based on experience. Accommodation support available for relocating candidates. Iqama sponsorship provided.

data.raw_snippet:

data.jd_text:
**About The Role**
**Grade Level (for internal use):**
12
  
  
**The Role:**
Head of KSA, Business Development Climate & Sustainability Services
  
  
**The Team:**
The candidate will be responsible for leading S&P Global’s Climate and Sustainability Services market expansion throughout the Kingdom, focusing on three primary client pillars: Financial Institutions, Non-Financial Corporates in hard-to-abate sectors, and Government Related Entities (GREs). This role demands deep client engagement and high-level strategic conversations, requiring the ability to connect across ministries, national champions, regulators, corporates, and financial institutions. Key expectations include building a robust on-the-ground network, meeting with senior executives and project owners, pitching tailored sustainability solutions, and designing offerings that leverage the full range of S&P Global’s data, analytics, platforms, and advisory services. The candidate will play a pivotal role in positioning S&P Global as a trusted partner in climate and sustainability, ensuring that client needs are met with innovative and impactful solutions.
  
  
**Responsibilities And Impact**

* Market Expansion & Positioning - Establish S&P Global’s sustainability and climate services in KSA and boost local market presence through ongoing engagement with ministries, corporates, and financial institutions. Build a strong network to enhance visibility and leadership.
* Client Engagement & Relationship Building - Cultivate lasting relationships with senior decision-makers and engage in informed discussions on climate, transition planning, ESG, and sustainability. Act as a strategic partner for continuous client engagement.
* Business Development & Growth - Pursue new opportunities with financial institutions, corporates, and government entities. Lead pitches and design tailored solutions utilizing S&P’s offerings. Lead internal teams in preparing proposals aligned with regional priorities.
* Solution Integration & Delivery Coordination - Turn client needs into actionable projects using data, analytics, and advisory platforms. Coordinate globally for seamless delivery and contribute to RHQ’s balanced onshore/offshore delivery model.

**What We’re Looking For**
**Basic Qualifications:**

* Exclusive for Saudi Nationals and be based in Riyadh to ensure essential on-the-ground presence
* 10+ years of experience in a consulting role, ideally covering banking, investment, risk, financing, or sustainability-related areas
* Demonstrate fluency in Arabic and English, with exceptional business writing and executive-level communication skills
* Proven experience building or scaling a business, team, or regional market presence
* Strong, hands-on experience in sustainability and climate topics, including advisory, technical analysis, or climate-related risk
* Deep technical knowledge of ISSB, PCAF, GRI, and decarbonization standards
* Experience with quantification of financed emissions, such as PCAF methodologies
* Familiarity with local industry dynamics in Saudi Arabia, especially hard-to-abate sectors
* Experience in Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions quantification
* Experience working in international organizations with exposure to global teams and matrix structures
* Demonstrated leadership experience, including managing teams, influencing stakeholders, and delivering cross-functional outcomes
* Strong project management capabilities, able to lead complex sustainability engagements end to end

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Deliver high-quality executive presentations and develop compelling materials using advanced PowerPoint skills
* Clearly and persuasively articulate complex sustainability concepts to diverse audiences
* Engage confidently with high-level stakeholders in both technical and strategic discussions
* Report directly to the Global Head of Climate and Sustainability Services.
* Contribute to the development of a hybrid delivery model by integrating onshore Riyadh-based staff with offshore and nearshore teams to support project execution
* Commit to regular travel within Riyadh; travel outside the Kingdom is limited and not a priority
* Drive business retention and expansion by strengthening client relationships and integrating data, analytics, services, and platforms to support account growth through upselling and cross-selling initiatives
* Lead the transition to recurring and subscription-based models by converting one-off projects into ongoing subscriptions and facilitating client adoption of S&P tools
* Identify and develop new key client opportunities by targeting financial institutions and government entities, expanding market presence in Saudi Arabia, and building a robust pipeline for sustainable growth
* This role is exclusively for Saudi nationals. Please note that we do not offer sponsorship or relocation for this position.

**About S&P Global Energy**
At S&P Global Energy, our comprehensive view of global energy and commodities markets enables our customers to make superior decisions and create long-term, sustainable value. Our four core capabilities are: Platts for news and pricing; CERA for research and advisory; Horizons for energy expansion and sustainability solutions; and Events for industry collaboration.
  
  
S&P Global Energy is a division of S&P Global (NYSE: SPGI). S&P Global enables businesses, governments, and individuals with trusted data, expertise, and technology to make decisions with conviction. We are Advancing Essential Intelligence through world-leading benchmarks, data, and insights that customers need in order to plan confidently, act decisively, and thrive economically in a rapidly changing global landscape. Learn more at www.spglobal.com/energy.
  
  
**What’s In It For**
**You?**
**Our Mission**
Advancing Essential Intelligence.
  
  
**Our People**
We're more than 35,000 strong worldwide—so we're able to understand nuances while having a broad perspective. Our team is driven by curiosity and a shared belief that Essential Intelligence can help build a more prosperous future for us all.From finding new ways to measure sustainability to analyzing energy transition across the supply chain to building workflow solutions that make it easy to tap into insight and apply it. We are changing the way people see things and empowering them to make an impact on the world we live in. We’re committed to a more equitable future and to helping our customers find new, sustainable ways of doing business. Join us and help create the critical insights that truly make a difference.
  
  
**Our Values**
**Integrity, Discovery, Partnership**
Throughout our history, the world's leading organizations have relied on us for the Essential Intelligence they need to make confident decisions about the road ahead. We start with a foundation of integrity in all we do, bring a spirit of discovery to our work, and collaborate in close partnership with each other and our customers to achieve shared goals.
  
  
**Benefits**
We take care of you, so you can take care of business. We care about our people. That’s why we provide everything you—and your career—need to thrive at S&P Global.
  
  
**Our Benefits Include**

* Health & Wellness: Health care coverage designed for the mind and body.
* Flexible Downtime: Generous time off helps keep you energized for your time on.
* Continuous Learning: Access a wealth of resources to grow your career and learn valuable new skills.
* Invest in Your Future: Secure your financial future through competitive pay, retirement planning, a continuing education program with a company-matched student loan contribution, and financial wellness programs.
* Family Friendly Perks: It’s not just about you. S&P Global has perks for your partners and little ones, too, with some best-in class benefits for families.
* Beyond the Basics: From retail discounts to referral incentive awards—small perks can make a big difference.

For more information on benefits by country visit: https://spgbenefits.com/benefit-summaries
  
  
**Global Hiring And Opportunity At S&P Global**
At S&P Global, we are committed to fostering a connected and engaged workplace where all individuals have access to opportunities based on their skills, experience, and contributions. Our hiring practices emphasize fairness, transparency, and merit, ensuring that we attract and retain top talent. By valuing different perspectives and promoting a culture of respect and collaboration, we drive innovation and power global markets.
  
  
**Recruitment Fraud Alert**
If you receive an email from a spglobalind.com domain or any other regionally based domains, it is a scam and should be reported to reportfraud@spglobal.com. S&P Global never requires any candidate to pay money for job applications, interviews, offer letters, “pre-employment training” or for equipment/delivery of equipment. Stay informed and protect yourself from recruitment fraud by reviewing our guidelines, fraudulent domains, and how to report suspicious activity here.
  
  
**Equal Opportunity Employer**
S&P Global is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to race/ethnicity, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, marital status, military veteran status, unemployment status, or any other status protected by law. Only electronic job submissions will be considered for employment.
  
  
If you need an accommodation during the application process due to a disability, please send an email to: EEO.Compliance@spglobal.com and your request will be forwarded to the appropriate person.
  
  
**US Candidates Only:**
The EEO is the Law Poster http://www.dol.gov/ofccp/regs/compliance/posters/pdf/eeopost.pdf describes discrimination protections under federal law. Pay Transparency Nondiscrimination Provision - https://www.dol.gov/sites/dolgov/files/ofccp/pdf/pay-transp\_%20English\_formattedESQA508c.pdf

data.title: Sales Associate - Riyadh based
data.raw_snippet:
**Lalamove**
is disrupting the logistics industry by connecting customers and drivers directly through our technology. We offer customers a lightning fast and convenient way to book delivery and moving services whether they are at their home, at work or on the go. People talk about O2O, we live it!
data.jd_text:
**Lalamove**
is disrupting the logistics industry by connecting customers and drivers directly through our technology. We offer customers a lightning fast and convenient way to book delivery and moving services whether they are at their home, at work or on the go. People talk about O2O, we live it!
  
  
Currently, Lalamove is a leading global on-demand delivery platform with millions of delivery partners serving millions of orders everyday.
  
  
With 1600+ employees spread across SEA, INDIA, LATAM, TURKEY, UAE and GERMANY; our company has reached unicorn status in 2018, is well funded by prominent VCs and has kept growing at tremendous speed since.
  
  
Our strength lies in our internal values, namely
**Passion**
in serving local communities, empowering SMEs and our driver partners,
**Execution**
and
**Grit**
because that is how we differentiate ourselves by never giving up and striving for excellence, and
**Humility**
- awareness in ourselves to learn from others and never stop improving.
  
  
At Lalamove, we strongly believe in the power of community. Millions of drivers and customers use our technology every day to connect with one another and move things that matter. Delivery is what we do best and we ensure it is always fast and simple. Since 2013, we have tackled the logistics industry head on to find the most innovative solutions for the world’s delivery needs.
  
  
Our vision is to bring communities closer and make city life easier by allowing fast and convenient circulation of goods. We realise this vision with a ‘glocal’ approach, building a robust operations team to adapt our product to local networks of businesses and delivery contractors. At the same time, we have ambition to build an international brand by establishing an even more global presence.
  
  
As part of our plan to achieve this ambition we will be entering the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, starting with Riyadh and seeking a Sales Associate based in Riyadh.
  
  
**What You'll Do**

* Sales and lead sourcing: Acquire and onboard business users by maintaining a healthy pipeline of leads as well as developing and taking care of professional relationships
* Salesmanship: Must have vast experience in sales in KSA, familiarity with the local market, and know how to prospect and qualify leads, build relationships and follow up on new business opportunities.
* Business development: Develop new business opportunities by establishing a strong working relationship with local partners.
* Sales operations: Ensure that the standard operating procedures and policies are implemented in a timely and accurate manner.
* Brand Ambassador: Become the face of Lalamove when engaging local partners to promote our services and values.

**What You'll Need**

* Minimum 2 years relevant sales working experience in KSA (startup or logistics industry is a plus).
* Bachelor's degree Business, Finance or related discipline from a reputable institution.
* Excellent problem solving, presentation and negotiation skills.
* Willingness to roll up your sleeves and take accountability.
* Must have excellent presentation and communications skills and be fluent in English, Arabic is a plus.
* Proficiency in CRM programs is a plus.
* Charming personality to build and maintain personal relationships.
* Autonomous and adaptable to cope with an ever-changing working environment.
* Can-do attitude and grit to make things happen, to identify and leverage new opportunities.
* Must have a valid driving license and car.

To all candidates- Lalamove respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data.
  
  
This Notice will inform you how we will use your personal data, explain your privacy rights and the protection you have by the law when you apply to join us. Please take time to read and understand this Notice. Candidate Privacy Notice: https://www.lalamove.com/en-sa/candidate-privacy-notice
  
  
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

data.title: Head of Revenue Operations MEA North
data.raw_snippet:
**We help the world run better**
At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'
data.jd_text:
**We help the world run better**
At SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed.
  
  
As a leader in the Go to Market Operations team, under our Office of the CEO’s Strategy & Operations organization, the MEA NORTH Revenue Operations Head is responsible for the planning, delivery, and execution of complex business operations processes across the entire Customer lifecycle. This leader will drive innovation, consistency, automation, and simplicity into the business to execute world-class field revenue operations in line with business goals, financial objectives, strategic imperatives, and field/organizational requirements. The MEA NORTH Revenue Operations Head will drive the MEA NORTH in alignment with our Regional Operational Excellence Framework, overall Transformation strategy and go-to-market evolution including (but not limited to) providing an execution playbook and supporting practices across MEA NORTH functions, channels, and business units along the End-to-End customer journey effecting a Cloud-centric operations culture and function across their MU.
  
  
**What You’ll Do**

* Lead the MEA NORTH Revenue Operations teams (supporting all phases of the Customer journey)
* Drive the entirety of Revenue Operations practices/cadences/KPISs across our ONE Customer Value Journey
* Provide insights to the business and leadership on Operational KPIs including but not limited to Coverage, Quota allocation & Attainment, Productivity, Forecast Accuracy. Develop and deploy corrective measures and best practices to meet and exceed KPI targets as well as work with the evolution of our Cloud metrics and business goals.
* Inspire and guide holistic organizational excellence. Drive the next generation of practices, processes and KPIs to deliver Customer Lifetime Value in the cloud in alignment with the Regional guidelines
* Thought Leadership to propose continuous innovation and optimization of the business operation function and / or drive organizational excellence at MEA NORTH level and share best practices Regionally and if required globally.
* Serve as a highly credible spokesperson for CS business operations initiatives in MEA NORTH Leverage innovative thinking with pragmatic execution to deliver world class Cloud-centric operations across all phases of the Customer journey.

**What You Bring**

* Leadership: Proven experience managing complex businesses and functions with a structured set of initiatives and programs.
* Transformation: Track record of translating transformations and strategy into executable deliverables and pragmatic operational outcomes delivering measurable business success (top / bottom line)
* Org & Operational Complexity: Demonstrated expert ability and readiness to lead in businesses/functions that require high degrees of complex cross-organizational interaction & collaboration under aggressive timelines and significant market pressure.
* Business Performance: Demonstrated track record leading customer-facing, sales, services, support or business operations organizations. Global sales, services, support or customer engagement leadership desired.
* Accountability & Cross-Cultural Experience: Able to successfully collaborate across cultures.
* Innovation: Drives operational innovation based on new CLTV model with a deep understanding of customer and organizational landscape & internal/external business factors.
* Brand Development: Positive internal & external brand, well-networked and well- positioned in MEA NORTH
* Proficient in Excel, data-driven tools, SAC, and data analysis.
* Experience in communicating with C-level executives.
* Sales experience, including forecasting, business and strategic planning, and performance management, is a plus.

**Leadership Competencies:**
The candidate must lead with expertise and innovation, inspiring and motivating through vision and influence. They should quickly adapt to challenges and maintain an optimistic outlook. Interaction with MEA NORTH management and exhibiting high integrity and ethics are crucial. Building a culture of accountability, transparency, and trust is vital, as well as gaining support for initiatives and navigating complex structures.
  
  
**Behavioral Competencies:**
The candidate should focus on customers and the company, demonstrating agility, flexibility under pressure, and emotional intelligence. Clear communication, both written and spoken, is essential. They must innovate, prioritize customer needs, and be well-networked. Developing leaders and promoting inclusion, collaboration, trust, and execution excellence are key. Integrity, alignment with company values, and valuing diversity in talent decisions are imperative.
  
  
**Bring out your best**
SAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.
  
  
**We win with inclusion**
SAP’s culture of inclusion, focus on health and well-being, and flexible working models help ensure that everyone – regardless of background – feels included and can run at their best. At SAP, we believe we are made stronger by the unique capabilities and qualities that each person brings to our company, and we invest in our employees to inspire confidence and help everyone realize their full potential. We ultimately believe in unleashing all talent and creating a better world.
  
  
SAP is committed to the values of Equal Employment Opportunity and provides accessibility accommodations to applicants with physical and/or mental disabilities. If you are interested in applying for employment with SAP and are in need of accommodation or special assistance to navigate our website or to complete your application, please send an e-mail with your request to Recruiting Operations Team: Careers@sap.com.
  
  
For SAP employees: Only permanent roles are eligible for the SAP Employee Referral Program, according to the eligibility rules set in the SAP Referral Policy. Specific conditions may apply for roles in Vocational Training.
  
  
**AI Usage in the Recruitment Process**
For information on the responsible use of AI in our recruitment process, please refer to our Guidelines for Ethical Usage of AI in the Recruiting Process.
  
  
Please note that any violation of these guidelines may result in disqualification from the hiring process.

data.raw_snippet:

This role will also cover the Boutique Manag
data.jd_text:
**MISSION**
The Deputy Boutique Manager is responsible for all aspects relating to boutique operations and administration including but not limited to reporting, retail operations, stock control, Health and Safety, security and performance analysis.
  
  
This role will also cover the Boutique Manager during any absences therefore a solid understanding of both front and back office boutique operations is essential.
  
  
He/She is in partnership with the Boutique Manager the warrant of the achievement of the business targets in the boutique by managing the boutique team, by ensuring excellence in boutique operations and client service.
  
  
**Key Responsabilities**
*Boutique Performance:*

* Co-monitor sales targets and KPI’s (defined by the Boutique Manager and the Brand) and co-implement annual action plans.
* Provide monthly reporting to your Boutique Manager (sales, traffic, KPIs, follow up, missed sales, stock issues, CS, discount…) and propose corrective actions.
* Run analytical report for the boutique when needed.
* Update boutique notice boards.

**Clients’ Treatment**

* Behave as an Ambassador of the Brand inside and outside the Boutique.
* Provide a “wow” treatment and the highest level of service and care to all customers.
* Handle with the highest professionalism all customer service and client issues and involve your Boutique Manager if needed.
* Implement an effective data capture & follow up system for clients, prospects, CS and reservations and make regular checks with the team. To be used during daily briefings.

**Team Co-management With Boutique Manager**

* Co-manage of Boutique staff and daily control of security guards.
* Co-animate daily brief according to guidelines and animate during Boutique Manager absences.
* Participate to “one to one” meetings with your sales staff.
* Co-evaluate your sales staff through the annual PMP process, fix their objectives and KPI’s according to guidelines and make regular assessments during the year.
* Delegate operational activities and establish clear responsibilities within your team.
* Co-animate your team with commercial actions (challenges, incentives, events) and information about the Brand’s activity.
* Transmit and control the relevant documents (invoices, third party commissions…) to your Boutique Manager for the calculation of individual monthly commissions and incentives.
* Assist Boutique Manager in providing training to the team and help in staff coaching when needed to make sure they have appropriate trainings to reach their targets in terms of sales, KPI’s.
* Make sure on a daily basis that the grooming guidelines are fully respected.
* Organize and monitor rotas/planning, vacations ensuring time optimization with business necessities.
* Co-develop a Business oriented approach and positive mindset in your boutique, through role plays, team building.

**Boutique Operations**

* Implement guidelines defined by your Boutique Manager and the Brand to ensure full compliance with sales, financial and security procedures.
* Ensure Boutique check list is fully implemented and that Brand’s guidelines are respected with no exception (merchandising, catering…).
* Analyze Qualitative Evaluation (Mystery shopping) feed backs with your Boutique Manager and set up corrective action plans.
* Co-organize CRM activities within the boutiques as per guidelines.
* Control of the quality of the data base per sales staff.
* Make sure daily traffic is correctly counted on a daily basis.
* Make sure your boutique is properly maintained (lights, furniture…) and take corrective actions.
* Ensure actively the implementation and adherence of Piaget procedures, standards and policies as communicated by the Maison.

**Stock Control**

* Responsible of replenishing stock according to Piaget procedures and policies.
* Maintain effective stock controls to ensure continued stock supply and ensure good management of the stock (Stock record accuracy).
* Take responsibility of designated stock/category, maintain the high standards and report any damages.
* Control the quality of the stock (scratches, movements, batteries for quartz watches…) and make sure all pieces are handled with extra care and in perfect selling conditions.
* Ensure deliveries are processed swiftly and that the boutique’s entire product assortment is made immediately available and easily accessible to clients on a consistent basis.
* Lead daily and annual stock inventories.
* Manage In and Out stock transfers and follow-up (Slip in, slip out, packaging) .
* Responsible for the after sale procedure and accomplishment of the boutique (faulty goods, returns, repairs).
* Organize safe and maintain a clean and neat stockroom and back office area.
* Replenish of all carrier bags, placing orders and communicating them to management.
* Maintain both cashiers desk replenished of all necessary stationary and keeping the cash desks clean and tidy at all times.
* Communicate any stock issues to management team in boutique and Head Office.
* Communicate to the sales team the delivery schedules.

**Visual Merchandising**

* Monitor actively the boutique team ensuring that merchandise presentation reflect VM standards and general tidiness and cleanliness of the boutique is maintained at all times.
* SKILLS:

**Professional**
A minimum of two to five years prior management experience and background in luxury activities (retail or service) is required.
  
  
Foreign languages required (English a must).
  
  
Computer knowledge in Microsoft; knowledge of SAP is a plus.
  
  
Shows innovation and initiative, be proactive and pre-empt boutique and team issues.
  
  
Excellent communication skills both written and verbal.
  
  
Successfully able to handle multiple demands and competing priorities in a multinational business.
  
  
Ability to complete projects in a timely manner.
  
  
**Personal**
Excellent Presentation.
  
  
Curiosity, passion for service and client orientation.
  
  
Excellent interpersonal, communication, organizational and problem-solving abilities.
  
  
Professionalism is maintained under all circumstances.

data.raw_snippet:
Lalamove is launching it's operations to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia - Riyadh
  
  
At Lalamove, we believe in the power of community. Millions of drivers and customers use our technology every day to connect with one another and move things that matter. Delivery is what we do best and we ensure it
data.jd_text:
Lalamove is launching it's operations to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia - Riyadh
  
  
At Lalamove, we believe in the power of community. Millions of drivers and customers use our technology every day to connect with one another and move things that matter. Delivery is what we do best and we ensure it is always fast and simple. Since 2013, we have tackled the logistics industry head on to find the most innovative solutions for the world’s delivery needs. We are full steam ahead to make Lalamove synonymous with delivery and on a mission to impact as many local communities as we can. We have massively scaled our efforts across Asia, Latin America, Middle East and now have our sights on taking our best in class technology to the rest of the world. And we need your help to get us there!
  
  
**Role**
As the
**Driver Operations Manager,**
you will be in charge of building and growing our supply for our KSA operations. Quick wits, problem-solving and a team-first mentality will be critical to your success. This role is a unique opportunity to have a measurable impact on your market through the growth and optimization of the driver population on the Lalamove platform.
  
  
**What You Will Do**

* Driver acquisition and onboarding: constantly maintain an over-supplying effort by keeping a healthy and efficient process for driver acquisition and onboarding.
* Community & Retention Management: Drive and lead communications, training, online community, engagement activities, and multiple business initiatives to build trust & loyalty with our driver-partners across KSA.
* Driver operations standards: identity, build and enforce Standard Operating Procedures and Policies to support the activity and solve or prevent issues.
* Performance: constantly track the efficiency of your team and the service quality provided to the network, and deploy corrective/improvement actions
* Team management: Hire, onboard, and train a team of driver operations leads and associates to execute the local operations; constantly track the efficiency of your team and the service quality provided to the network, and deploy corrective/improvement actions
* Process improvement and standards: Identity, build and enforce standard operating procedures to manage the supply teams effectively

**What You'll Need**

* Degree holder with at least 6 years of operational/leadership experience, preferably in a logistics/e-commerce fulfillment environment
* Team management experience with at least 3 direct reports, plus if have experience leading multiple functions/verticals
* Experience in managing complex, cross-functional projects, plus if have experience working closely with the headquarter (Hong Kong) teams
* Knowledge and understanding of mobile and web technologies
* Positive & open-minded, result-oriented with strong business acumen
* Good command of both written and spoken English and Arabic

To all candidates- Lalamove respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data.
  
  
This Notice will inform you how we will use your personal data, explain your privacy rights and the protection you have by the law when you apply to join us. Please take time to read and understand this Notice. Candidate Privacy Notice: https://www.lalamove.com/en-sa/candidate-privacy-notice
  
  
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

data.raw_snippet:

Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre-sa
data.jd_text:
**About This Opportunity**
Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre-sales and overlooks integrated delivery models setup including System Integration, Application Development & Maintenance , and IT Managed Services.
  
  
This role reports to the Head of SL BOS Delivery Excellence for MEMA and will be part of BOS Delivery Excellence Leadership team.
  
  
What You Will Do:
  
  

* Lead BOS service excellence, managing team of senior services professionals.
* Accountable for services engagement and pre-sales including target topline and profitability.
* Responsible for aligning with Solution Area on Services GTM and drive services offering build up , presales and delivery engagements for the EMEA region.
* Secure service delivery readiness through competence planning inline with business and portfolio evolution.
* Senior People Manager of own unit and a senior Leader across matrixed organization extending to solution line professionals in other CU BOS, BOS solution lines and SD/SDU units.
* Adopt, position and expand integrated service delivery offerings; with focus on customer value realization, delivery efficiency, and adapting to different commercial models.
* Champion delivery efficiency initiatives in collaboration with program management, CU BOS SL and SD/SDU.
* Set Individual Performance and Development goals for members with regular check-ins.
* Responsible for team dimensioning; and Cost Center Responsible to deliver over-Absorption target and required Utilization/Billability.
* Drive a Knowledge Sharing and Asset building culture across their community.
* Instill a culture of add-on sales and customer value maximization.

What You Will Bring:
  
  

* 12+ years of experience in telecom industry with leadership track record in professional services including system integration, application development & maintenance, and IT managed services.
* Deep understanding of BOS portfolio including products and services.
* University degree in ICT/Engineering. A graduate degree such as an MBA is a plus.
* Strong People Manager track record leading Senior Team members and leading Leaders.
* Deep Telecom knowledge including understanding of Telecom networks, OSS & BSS components and familiarity with TmForum (eTOM, SID, TAM)
* Advanced experience with IT Systems and Integration
* OSS and BSS full stack integrations and surrounding ecosystems
* API management & microservice architecture
* Cloud-Native & Platform transformation
* Data analytics, AI/ML applications in service operations
* In depth knowledge of different SDLC methodologies including Agile, Waterfall and DevOps approaches.
* Proven experience with MS IT and ADM related frameworks – ITIL, COBIT & SAFe
* Demonstrated experience leading cross-functional highly matrixed and globally distributed teams.
* Recommended certifications (external): ITIL, Lean Six Sigma, PMP
* Recommended certifications (internal): MSCOO, ICT PD JS8 GIAP certification
* Strong consultative, presentation, and communication skills
* Proven track record to deliver results within a time sensitive and high-pressure environment.
* Highly proficient collaboration skills
* Solid ability to communicate effectively at executive levels.
* In-depth knowledge of running Organizational and Solution Level Governance.
* Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.

Note: There are a total of 4 Head of Integration Services Hub vacancies with the same responsibility and skills requirements.
  
  
Ericsson South Africa recruits in line with its Employment Equity plan and will therefore give preference to suitably qualified candidates who are members of designated groups. Would you like to take a tour to “Life at Ericsson MEA”, visit short video Click Here
  
  
**Why join Ericsson?**
At Ericsson, you´ll have an outstanding opportunity. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next.
  
  
**What happens once you apply?**
Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like.
  
  
Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more.

776664

data.jd_text:
**Tech Director (Head of Engineering)**
**Globant — Riyadh, KSA**
**Location:**
Riyadh, Saudi Arabia (on-site). Alternative locations: Jordan, Lebanon, UAE, Egypt — with regular travel to Riyadh required.
  
  
**Reports to:**
VP of Technology
  
  
**Direct scope:**
50+ engineers across back end and front end disciplines
  
  
**Role type:**
New role | Permanent | Leadership/Strategy
  
  
**About The Role**
This is a senior engineering leadership position responsible for the strategic direction, delivery performance, and technical maturity of Globant's software engineering function in the MENA region. The Head of Engineering will own the engineering roadmap, drive AI adoption, establish engineering culture and standards, and lead a growing organisation of 50+ engineers across back end and front end teams.
  
  
**Key Priorities**

* Improve delivery velocity — Identify and remove bottlenecks across the engineering lifecycle. Establish metrics, streamline release processes, and improve predictability of delivery across teams.
* Drive AI adoption across engineering — Define and execute a strategy for embedding AI into engineering workflows, tooling, and product development. Champion the use of AI-assisted development, testing, and operations to accelerate output and quality.
* Build engineering culture and processes — Establish engineering standards, governance, and ways of working. Improve tooling, development processes, and code standards across teams. Create a culture of ownership, technical excellence, and continuous improvement. Introduce career frameworks, knowledge sharing, and communities of practice.
* Tackle technical debt and platform modernisation — Assess the current estate, prioritise remediation, and lead modernisation efforts aligned with long-term architectural goals. Balance delivery demands with sustainable engineering practices.
* Scale the engineering organisation — Partner with talent acquisition to grow the team in line with business demand. Build a hiring strategy across Riyadh and alternative locations (Jordan, Lebanon, UAE, Egypt), ensuring quality and cultural alignment.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Set the technical vision and engineering strategy aligned with business objectives
* Lead and develop engineering managers, SMEs, and senior engineers across all disciplines
* Own engineering delivery performance, reporting, and continuous improvement
* Collaborate closely with architecture, cybersecurity, product, delivery, design, QA, data, and DevSecOps/platform disciplines to ensure alignment and cohesive outcomes across the engineering organisation
* Represent engineering in senior leadership forums and client engagements
* Manage stakeholder relationships across the organisation and with external partners
* Champion modern engineering practices including DevSecOps, CI/CD, observability, and automation
* Ensure compliance with security, governance, and regulatory requirements

**Technical Landscape**
The role provides strategic oversight across the following technology stack. Hands-on coding is not expected, but a strong understanding of the technologies in use and the ability to engage credibly with technical teams is required.
  
  
**Back End:**
NodeJS, Adobe Experience Manager, Apigee, RESTful APIs, microservices architecture, event-driven architecture, SQL and NoSQL databases, OAuth/JWT, message queuing and async processing.
  
  
**Front End:**
Flutter, Svelte, HTML5/CSS3/JavaScript/TypeScript, Android and iOS native development, component-based architecture, micro frontend patterns, progressive web applications, accessibility (WCAG), state management.
  
  
**AI & Emerging Tech:**
AI-assisted development tooling, Agentic AI, LLMOps, AI integration into engineering workflows, evaluation of emerging AI/ML technologies for engineering productivity.
  
  
**Requirements**

* Significant experience leading engineering organisations of 50+ in a Tech/SaaS environment
* Proven track record of improving delivery velocity and engineering maturity at scale
* Experience introducing AI tooling and practices into engineering teams
* Strong understanding of back end and front end technologies — able to engage credibly with engineering teams and contribute to technical discussions
* Demonstrated ability to work effectively across disciplines including architecture, cybersecurity, product, delivery, design, and QA
* Experience building engineering teams across multiple geographies, ideally in the MENA region
* Strong stakeholder management and communication skills with senior/executive audiences
* Experience with engineering governance, standards, and platform modernisation
* Track record of building engineering culture, career frameworks, and communities of practice

**Desirable**

* Experience in a consulting or digital services environment
* Experience working with Saudi Arabia's digital transformation landscape (e.g. Vision 2030 aligned programmes)

data.title: Head of Product - TradePay
data.raw_snippet:
