data.title: Business Development Manager
data.raw_snippet:
Overview:

The Business Development Manager supports ADC’s growth agenda by identifying new business opportunities, strengthening strategic relationships, and supporting market expansion initiatives. Reporting to the Director, this role focuses on executing approved business development strategies,
data.jd_text:
Overview:

The Business Development Manager supports ADC’s growth agenda by identifying new business opportunities, strengthening strategic relationships, and supporting market expansion initiatives. Reporting to the Director, this role focuses on executing approved business development strategies, managing selected client and partner relationships, and contributing to revenue growth across ADC’s connectivity and digital solutions portfolio.

The role combines commercial analysis, opportunity development, stakeholder engagement, and cross-functional coordination to drive measurable business outcomes.

Key Responsibilities:

•Support the execution of ADC’s business development strategy in alignment with commercial objectives.

•Identify and assess new business opportunities across target markets and industry verticals.

•Develop business cases, commercial proposals, and partnership models under the guidance of the Director.

•Maintain and manage a qualified opportunity pipeline to support revenue targets.

•Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify growth areas and emerging trends.

•Support evaluation of strategic partnerships and collaboration opportunities.

•Provide data-driven insights to refine go-to-market approaches and commercial positioning.

•Manage day-to-day relationships with assigned clients and partners.

•Support negotiations and commercial discussions within delegated authority.

•Coordinate with internal stakeholders to ensure timely responses to client and partner requirements.

•Work closely with Sales, Product, Strategy, and Finance teams to align opportunity development efforts.

•Ensure smooth transition of secured opportunities to Sales or Delivery teams.

•Support preparation of executive materials and reports for leadership reviews.

•Track business development KPIs including pipeline growth, opportunity conversion, and revenue contribution.

•Prepare periodic progress reports and performance updates for the Director.

•Monitor market developments and competitor activities, providing structured insights.

•Represent ADC in selected industry events, meetings, and networking forums.

•Support initiatives that enhance ADC’s market visibility and brand positioning.

Qualifications:

Education:

•Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Telecommunications, Engineering, or a related field

Experience:

•4+ years of experience in business development, sales, partnerships, or commercial strategy.

•Experience in telecom, connectivity, or technology sectors preferred.

•Demonstrated experience supporting revenue generation initiatives and managing stakeholder relationships.

•Exposure to commercial modeling and proposal development is an advantage.

Skills & Competencies:

•Commercial & Analytical Thinking

•Opportunity Assessment

•Negotiation Support Skills

•Communication & Presentation Skills

data.title: Senior Delivery Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**Senior Delivery Manager**

* **FinTech - Bahrain (Onsite)**

A growing fintech in Bahrain is looking for someone to bring more structure and control to the way software gets delivered.

They’re building customer-facing products in a regulated environment, while also putting the internal foundation
data.jd_text:
**Senior Delivery Manager**

* **FinTech - Bahrain (Onsite)**

A growing fintech in Bahrain is looking for someone to bring more structure and control to the way software gets delivered.

They’re building customer-facing products in a regulated environment, while also putting the internal foundations in place for the next stage of growth. The engineering team is growing, priorities are moving quickly, and they need someone who can create clarity across the work, keep delivery on track, and make sure the wider business has confidence in what’s happening.

This is a role for someone who is organised, proactive, and comfortable sitting in the middle of moving parts.

**The Role:**

We’re supporting a client hiring a
**Senior Delivery Manager**
to help tighten up delivery across the engineering function.

This is not a role for someone who only wants to run stand-ups and update tickets. They need someone who can bring shape to the day-to-day, keep teams aligned, surface issues early, and make it easier for engineering, product, and leadership to stay on the same page.

You’ll be working across different areas of the tech team and helping create a more joined-up way of planning, tracking, and communicating work. If things are unclear, you bring clarity. If something is drifting, you spot it early. If leadership asks where things stand, you can give a straight answer.

**What You’ll Be Doing:**

* Driving day-to-day delivery coordination across the engineering team.
* Keeping work visible, organised, and moving in the right direction.
* Working closely with engineering and product to improve planning and follow-through.
* Making sure blockers, risks, and dependencies are surfaced early.
* Helping teams stay aligned on priorities, timelines, and ownership.
* Building a clearer reporting rhythm so leadership can see progress without chasing updates.
* Supporting better sprint planning, estimation, and delivery habits over time.
* Facilitating the meetings that matter and keeping them useful.
* Using data, trends, and delivery patterns to improve how the team operates.
* Helping put better structure in place as the company continues to grow.

**What’s In It For You:**

* A role with real visibility and real influence.
* The chance to help shape how a scaling fintech delivers software.
* Direct interaction with decision-makers across the business.
* A fast-moving environment where good work is noticed quickly.
* The opportunity to build structure that actually improves how people work day to day.

**What You’ll Need to Succeed:**

* 5+ years in delivery, project, or technical program management
* Experience working closely with software engineers and product stakeholders.
* Strong organisational skills and the ability to stay on top of multiple moving pieces.
* Confidence bringing structure into environments that are still evolving.
* A proactive mindset, you don’t wait for problems to become obvious.
* Strong communication skills and the ability to keep both technical and non-technical people aligned.
* Comfort working in agile environments, but with a practical approach rather than rigid process.
* A good eye for where delivery is getting stuck and what needs to change.
* Enough technical understanding to follow engineering discussions and ask the right questions.
* A calm, hands-on style and the credibility to work across teams.

**Nice to Have:**

* Experience in fintech, financial services, or another regulated environment.
* Exposure to startup or scale-up businesses.
* Familiarity with tools like Jira, Linear, and GitHub.
* An understanding of modern engineering workflows and release cycles.
* Arabic language skills.
* A technical background or some previous coding exposure.

**Next Steps:**

If you’re good at bringing order to moving parts, keeping teams coordinated, and helping software get delivered with more confidence and less noise, this is worth a look.

The client is keen to hire someone who can come in, take ownership, and make a difference quickly.

*nineDots is a specialist technology & outsourcing recruitment agency advertising this role on behalf of our client.*

data.title: Interface Stakeholder Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Description**
WSP in the Middle East is currently hiring for an Interface & Stakeholder Manager – Aviation Projects to join our team in KSA/ UAE. The Interface & Stakeholder Manager will be responsible for managing all project interfaces across aviation ‑ sector stakeholders, including governm
data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
WSP in the Middle East is currently hiring for an Interface & Stakeholder Manager – Aviation Projects to join our team in KSA/ UAE. The Interface & Stakeholder Manager will be responsible for managing all project interfaces across aviation ‑ sector stakeholders, including governmental authorities, airport operators, contractors, consultants, utility providers, and internal technical teams. The role ensures seamless coordination, clear communication, and the timely resolution of interface issues that may affect programme delivery. It also involves developing and implementing structured stakeholder engagement plans to maintain alignment, avoid conflicts, and support smooth project execution. The Manager will provide regular reporting to the Project Director, highlighting interface risks, dependencies, and mitigation strategies critical to the success of the aviation programme.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Lead and manage all interface activities across aviation stakeholders (government bodies, airport operators, contractors, consultants, utilities, and internal teams).
* Establish and maintain effective communication channels to ensure seamless coordination between all project parties.
* Identify, track, and resolve interface issues that could affect project progress, technical delivery, or schedule performance.
* Develop, implement, and maintain structured Stakeholder Engagement Plans to ensure alignment throughout all project phases.
* Facilitate interface workshops, coordination meetings, and technical discussions with project stakeholders.
* Liaise with regulatory authorities and airport operators to ensure compliance with aviation and infrastructure requirements.
* Prepare regular interface status reports and provide interface ‑ risk updates to the Project Director and leadership team.
* Maintain a comprehensive interface register, ensuring risks, dependencies, and actions are clearly documented and managed.
* Support integration between design, procurement, and construction teams, ensuring all disciplines are aligned.

Promote a collaborative working environment across multidisciplinary teams
  
  
**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Project Management, or a relevant field.
* 20+ years of experience in interface management, stakeholder management, or project coordination within large ‑ scale aviation or major infrastructure projects.
* Experience working with airport authorities, regulatory bodies, or large PMC/consultancy organisations (highly preferred).
* Strong technical understanding of aviation infrastructure, design processes, construction interfaces, and multi ‑ party coordination.
* Proven track record resolving complex interface issues in fast ‑ paced project environments.

Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all.
  
  
Join our close-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the world.
  
  
**With us, you can**
. Apply today.

data.title: Software Engineering Director
data.raw_snippet:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
  
  
Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together u
data.jd_text:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
  
  
Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together under a single director of engineering. Once you have passed the first round of interviews we will discuss specific software products which would be a good fit for your interests.
  
  
We believe that open source is just starting to transform the tech sector and enterprise compute. Our goal is to make open source easier, more reliable and more secure for deployment and development. We strive to be the provider of 'most software to most companies'. To deliver on that ambition, our engineers are carefully selected from the applicants across the globe. We select for brilliance and motivation to take open source to the next level. Our engineering directors set the pace and the direction to help teams achieve more than they realised they could, and feel proud of the result.
  
  
Even though this is a senior management position, managing managers, we expect Engineering Directors to be outstanding developers with the confidence to lead by example across the full range of activities in their teams. You need to understand what great code looks like in the language and genre of your product - whether that is a web service, or a system daemon, or a desktop application, or a distributed system. You'll need to know how to drive testing and benchmarking to improve the clarity in your teams around quality and performance. You'll also need to be confident to set and defend high expectations of work ethic, consistency, focus and learning.
  
  
We have director level roles across a wide range of engineering domains, including:
  
  

* Python and Golang
* C / C++ / Rust
* Data infrastructure
* HTML / CSS / JavaScript / Typescript / React
* Flutter
* Distro packaging and systems
* SAAS and web microservices
* Kernel
* Servers
* Graphics, Browser and Desktop
* Silicon enablement and embedded devices
* Product Security

If your domain of expertise isn't listed above, yet you feel it's relevant to Canonical, then feel free to apply anyway. We will route you to the most suitable team.
  
  
Location: The role is remote, with positions available in all time zones. It includes a requirement for global travel for twice per year, for trips up to two weeks long.
  
  
What you'll do
  
  

* Lead multiple teams of engineers, ranging from graduate to senior
* Develop your engineering managers and maintain Canonical culture
* Hire staff level engineers for specific roles like performance and quality
* Coach, mentor, and offer career development feedback
* Identify and measure indicators of team health and productivity
* Ensure rounded delivery including community participation and docs
* Implement disciplined engineering processes and defend them
* Set and measure progress against benchmark goals for speed and efficiency
* Represent your product to stakeholders, partners, and customers
* Develop and evangelise great engineering and organisational practices
* Plan and manage progress on agreed goals and projects
* Be an active part of the leadership team, collaborating with other leaders
* Collaborate with leads for related or dependent products

What we're looking for in you
  
  

* An exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
* Undergraduate degree in Computer Science or STEM, or a compelling narrative about your alternative path
* Extensive management experience and confidence to set high expectations
* Outstanding Linux based software engineering track record
* Drive and a track record of going above-and-beyond expectations
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
* Public speaking and presentation skills
* A love of developing and growing people and a track record of it
* Experience in leading, coaching and mentoring software developers
* Organised and able to ensure your team delivers timely, high quality results
* Well-organised, self-starting and able to deliver to schedule
* Professional manner interacting with colleagues, partners, and community
* You have advanced expertise in your own domain
* You are knowledgeable and passionate about software development
* You have solid experience working in an agile development environment
* You have a demonstrated drive for continual learning
* Builds trust, relationships and confidence
* Result-oriented, with a personal drive to meet commitments
* Ability to travel twice a year, for company events up to two weeks each

**Additional Skills We Value**

* Experience in a developer advocacy or community role
* Open source community engagement and leadership
* Ops and system administration experience
* Performance engineering and security experience

What we offer you
  
  
We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
  
  

* Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events

**About Canonical**
Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Canonical has been a remote-first company since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
  
  
Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
  
  
We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

data.title: Parttime Consultant
data.raw_snippet: We are looking for Professional Consultants for KFUPM Master Plan. The King Fahd University of Petroleum and Minerals is seeking Consultants for Professional Project Management and Technical Support Services for the KFUPM Master Plan Project. The scope basically comprises of supervisory and coordina
data.jd_text:
We are looking for Professional Consultants for KFUPM Master Plan. The King Fahd University of Petroleum and Minerals is seeking Consultants for Professional Project Management and Technical Support Services for the KFUPM Master Plan Project. The scope basically comprises of supervisory and coordination work with regard to master planning and implementation exercises. The hired individuals or Consultants shall envision the future of KFUPM in the next 25 years and also guide KFUPM in developing the Vision by assessing the proposed policies and strategic changes, assist in budget preparation and prioritizing improvements in community services.

1. Project Manager (preferably 15 years of experience): Shall be an Urban and Regional Planner who has Masters Degree in urban planning and has previously worked on managing master plan projects and has experience in sustainability. Having PHD would be an added advantage. Skilled in organizing, prioritizing, and scheduling workflow. Writing high quality reports with recommendations. Lead project teams, build relationships and manage community engagements. Technically review and suggest best practices for the different aspects of the master plan and sustainability. Attend, manage, and take minutes to ensure compliance of Master Planners to adhere to schedule of deliverables, and full coordination between all disciplines. Align lessons learned during surveys, interviews and field trips with goals and needs, and adjust to meet cultural requirements. Project Management during the execution phases to ensure client requirements are fulfilled.

2. Electro-Mechanical Consultant (preferably 10 years of experience): Shall have a Bachelor’s degree in relevant engineering discipline like mechanical or electrical with preferable master’s degree. Analyze problems and create innovative solutions by applying technical knowledge. Supervise and analyze the conditional/performance assessment of electro-mechanical utilities and systems in buildings and networks. Guide in developing technically excellent engineering solutions in the built environment. Capable to understand, inspect and report about the systems and networks related to Electrical and Mechanical engineering, Public Health, Heating Ventilation and Air Conditioning, Sustainability, Stormwater, Wastewater, Waste-recycling, Fire and Security, Building Management Systems and IT and Vertical Transportation. Able to handedly manage in preparation and execution of electro-mechanical systems’ retrofits/upgrades as per latest energy efficient technology. Always focus on energy conservation measures in all aspects of the work.

3. GIS Specialist (preferably 10 years of experience): Shall have a Bachelor’s degree in a discipline such as Urban Planning, Geography, or Engineering. Profound GIS knowledge with preferably a master’s degree in relevant discipline. As a GIS specialist is expected to work with various GIS tools. Supervising the development and management of GIS applications that are being developed by the master planner. Efficiently Maintaining and updating the geospatial database. Developing effective maps and aerial photography. Carry out planning and coordination of GIS activities to meet the outlined goals. Able to handle advanced Arc GIS tools in operating smart digitized maps of the Campus. Have knowledge of Digital Twins, VR, AI and advanced technologies alike. Create procedures and keep updating the maps as per changes requested and as-built information.

4.Stakeholder Engagement Officer (preferably 7 years of experience): Shall a Business or Literature Graduate with preferably master’s degree in relevant field. The candidate shall have experience in assisting executive personnel in stakeholder management. Responsible to facilitate collaboration between all the KFUPM stakeholders, Consultant and the Master Planner. Manage regular contact with all stakeholders to keep them informed of project activities, collaborative actions, and needs from the project. Can work along with PMO in establishing a very good spoken and written communication, for sharing information, scheduling and record-keeping. Able to prioritize the correspondences and coordinating for the timely responses from the stakeholders. Maintaining a good record of all the stakeholder engagements.

5. Project Management Officer (preferably 10 years of experience): Shall be a bachelor’s degree in any relevant discipline with previous experience of working with higher management. Work exclusively under the KFUPM Project Director to assist project related administration, coordination and communication. Also assist the Consultant project manager in preparation of progress presentations. Achieve significant productivity in coordinating project tasks with various trades. Maintain and manage project documentation.

The Consultants annual contract shall be renewed for a maximum of 2 consecutive years based on the performance and for the part-time consultants it will be renewed for a maximum of 1 year based on the performance.

data.title: Design Director – Airport
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Description**
WSP in the Middle East is currently hiring for a Design Director- Airport to join our team in Dammam, Eastern Province, KSA.
  
  
Reporting to the Project Director, the Design Director leads all airport design management activities across disciplines, ensuring technical excellen
data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
WSP in the Middle East is currently hiring for a Design Director- Airport to join our team in Dammam, Eastern Province, KSA.
  
  
Reporting to the Project Director, the Design Director leads all airport design management activities across disciplines, ensuring technical excellence and full compliance with programme requirements. The role provides strategic oversight of design quality, innovation, design integration, and design‑related risk mitigation. The Design Director will coordinate multidisciplinary design teams, manage design progress, and ensure seamless integration of design deliverables with construction, operational, and stakeholder needs. They also serve as the primary interface for resolving complex technical issues across the project.
  
  
**Responsibilities**
**Design Leadership & Oversight**

* Lead and manage all design activities across architectural, structural, airside, landside, MEP, and specialist disciplines.
* Drive technical excellence, consistency, and alignment with WSP design standards and aviation best practices.
* Ensure design deliverables fully align with project objectives, scope, and regulatory requirements.

**Multidisciplinary Design Management**

* Coordinate and supervise multidisciplinary design teams, ensuring timely progress of all design packages.
* Oversee integration of design outputs with procurement, construction, commissioning, and airport operational needs.
* Review and validate technical submissions, drawings, specifications, and design reports.

**Stakeholder & Client Coordination**

* Act as the main technical interface with the client, authorities, airport operators, and key stakeholders.
* Facilitate design workshops, technical meetings, and design reviews to resolve issues and align expectations.
* Provide clear, authoritative guidance on design matters to support informed decision ‑ making.

**Quality, Safety & Compliance**

* Champion design quality, innovation, sustainability, and compliance with local and international aviation codes.
* Implement quality assurance processes aligned with WSP global design standards.
* Ensure design solutions meet safety, regulatory, and operational requirements throughout the project lifecycle.

**Risk & Change Management**

* Identify design risks early, propose mitigation strategies, and manage design ‑ related changes effectively.
* Support the Project Director in managing scope, change orders, and design ‑ related commercial impacts.

**Team Leadership & Collaboration**

* Mentor and guide design managers, discipline leads, and wider technical teams.
* Promote a collaborative environment across WSP offices in KSA, UAE, India, and other regions.
* Ensure resource planning and performance management support project delivery demands.

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree in architecture or engineering (Master’s degree preferred).
* 15–20+ years’ experience in airport or major infrastructure design delivery.
* Strong background leading multidisciplinary design teams in PMC or consultancy environments.
* Experience with airport terminal design, airside/landside infrastructure, or large ‑ scale aviation modernisation projects (KSA experience advantageous).

**Technical Competencies**

* Expert understanding of aviation design standards, ICAO codes, NFPA, and relevant regulatory frameworks.
* Strong design integration and coordination capability across disciplines.
* Skilled in design review, technical validation, and risk ‑ based decision ‑ making.

**Behavioural Competencies**

* Strong leadership presence with excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
* Solution ‑ driven, proactive, and resilient under pressure.
* Ability to influence senior stakeholders on technical and strategic matters.

data.title: Special Airport Systems – Passive Engineer
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Description**
WSP in the Middle East is seeking an experienced Special Airport Systems (SAS) – Passive Engineer to be based in Dammam, KSA.
  
  
This role leads and oversees all passive infrastructure elements supporting Special Airport Systems, including structured cabling, pathways, contain
data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
WSP in the Middle East is seeking an experienced Special Airport Systems (SAS) – Passive Engineer to be based in Dammam, KSA.
  
  
This role leads and oversees all passive infrastructure elements supporting Special Airport Systems, including structured cabling, pathways, containment systems, and associated infrastructure. You will ensure passive systems are designed, installed, tested, and commissioned in line with project standards and aviation requirements, while coordinating closely with active systems, ICT, and construction interfaces. You will also support the Project Director by monitoring progress and ensuring integration with airport operational frameworks.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Lead the passive SAS scope across the project lifecycle: design review, shop drawing/submittal review, installation oversight, testing, and handover.
* Own passive infrastructure engineering deliverables including cable routing, containment coordination, pathway planning, and integration across disciplines.
* Coordinate with active SAS teams, ICT, and construction stakeholders to ensure seamless interface management and end-to-end system readiness.
* Ensure installation quality and compliance through inspections, method statement/ITP reviews, and progress verification against programme milestones.
* Drive commissioning readiness: support pre-commissioning checks, FAT/SAT preparation where applicable, integrated testing coordination, and documentation closure.
* Manage technical queries (RFIs), site issues, and resolution tracking to prevent delays and ensure compliance with airport/aviation expectations.
* Monitor progress, highlight risks, and provide clear reporting to the Project Director, supporting decisions and mitigation planning.
* Ensure documentation quality for handover, including as-builts, test results, O&M inputs, and evidence packs aligned to project requirements.

**Qualifications**

* 15+ years of relevant experience in passive infrastructure delivery for complex ICT/ELV/airport/mission-critical environments.
* Strong knowledge of structured cabling systems, containment (tray/trunking/conduit), pathways, and passive infrastructure coordination in live construction settings.
* Proven experience working on major multi-stakeholder projects, interfacing with active systems and ICT teams and managing construction constraints.
* Solid capability in quality and progress control: inspections, compliance checks, test planning, and structured close-out.
* Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to provide concise, accurate progress and risk updates.
* A practical, site-oriented mindset with strong problem-solving ability and attention to technical detail.

data.title: Area Head of MPL - SAA
data.raw_snippet:
**About US**
A.P. Moller - Maersk is an integrated container logistics company working to connect and simplify its customers’ supply chains. As the global leader in shipping services, the company operates in 130 countries and employs roughly 76,000 people.
  
  
With simple end-to-end offering of pr
data.jd_text:
**About US**
A.P. Moller - Maersk is an integrated container logistics company working to connect and simplify its customers’ supply chains. As the global leader in shipping services, the company operates in 130 countries and employs roughly 76,000 people.
  
  
With simple end-to-end offering of products and digital services, seamless customer engagement and a superior end-to-end delivery network, Maersk enables its customers to trade and grow by transporting goods anywhere - all over the world.
  
  
As Area Head of Maersk Project Logistics - Saudi Arabia Area, you will be accountable for operational performance and profitable growth within the Area/Cluster. Leads operations and business development teams
  
  
to deliver service excellence, execute global standards, drive commercial opportunities, and own the P&L for their defined scope. Ensures
  
  
alignment between product, sales, and operations to secure customer outcomes and sustainable growth. This role reports to Regional Head of MPL - IMEA.
  
  
**Key responsibilities:**
**Leadership & team management**

* Leads Area/Cluster operations and business development teams (incl. managers, specialists, execution staff).
* Builds capabilities, drives performance management, and ensures a strong team culture.

**Operational execution**

* Ensures end-to-end execution quality, service delivery, compliance, and QHSE adherence.
* Monitors operational KPIs, identifies root causes of issues, and drives timely recovery actions.
* Oversees resource planning, workload distribution, and utilisation of tools, platforms, and processes.
* Leads implementation of new solutions, product updates, systems, and operational standards.

**Commercial & business development**

* Drives Area/Cluster growth through opportunity qualification, pipeline support, and customer solutioning.
* Partners with Area LT and Sales to retain and grow business through improved delivery, onboarding, and customer experience.
* Leads operational and commercial input into RFPs, pricing, and customer engagement.

**Governance & performance management**

* Owns Area/Cluster P&L for their operational scope.
* Conducts regular performance reviews and forecasting with regional leadership.
* Ensures adherence to global standards, policies, and processes and compliance with QHSE policies.
* Drives continuous improvement, cycle-time reduction, and operational optimisation.

**Stakeholder alignment**

* Acts as primary operational & commercial-enablement representative for the Area/Cluster.
* Ensures alignment across Product, Sales, CX, Finance, Procurement, and Regional Ops.
* Represents the function in escalations, customer meetings, and strategic reviews.

**Skills required:**

* Strong logistics experience
* Extensive management experience with past performance across multiple geographies
* Experience with and sustained interest in systems and process management, process improvement
* Good collaborator and communicator
* In-depth understanding of Project Logistics delivery
* Strong analytical ability with a global, digital mindset
* Strong collaborative drive and stakeholder management skills
* Extensive supply chain experience.

Maersk is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we embrace different styles of thinking. Maersk is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applicants without regard to race, colour, gender, sex, age, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy or parental leave, veteran status, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with all legal requirements.
  
  
We are happy to support your need for any adjustments during the application and hiring process. If you need special assistance or an accommodation to use our website, apply for a position, or to perform a job, please contact us by emailing accommodationrequests@maersk.com.

data.title: Admin Support
data.raw_snippet:
**About The Job Admin Support**
Grant Thornton Bahrain is seeking a highly organized and efficient Admin Support to join our team in Seef, Bahrain. As an Admin Support, you will be responsible for providing administrative and clerical support to our team, ensuring the smooth and efficient operation
data.jd_text:
**About The Job Admin Support**
Grant Thornton Bahrain is seeking a highly organized and efficient Admin Support to join our team in Seef, Bahrain. As an Admin Support, you will be responsible for providing administrative and clerical support to our team, ensuring the smooth and efficient operation of our office.
  
  
**Roles And Responsibilities**

* Prepare audit proposals, engagement letters, deliverables (financials), Legal Department documents etc; and ensure all these are duly signed by the respective department head and updated on server.
* Organize dispatch documents (for all documents received before 10am daily)
* Attach transmittal sheets to all deliveries
* Coordinate delivery of documents to each department or clients (schedule for Drivers)
* Record transmittal / confirmation of delivery
* Inform concerned Manager and support staff by 5PM of undelivered/ uncollected documents with reasons.
* Maintain deliveries logbook
* File engagement letters, invoices, cheque copies and receipts in client file
* Monitor client file log to track movement of files
* Ensure all files are up to date and complete
* Proper storage and arrangement of archived files
* Manage client folders on server

**Qualifications & Skills Required**

* Bachelor's degree in business administration and accounting/finance
* 1 to 2 years of relevant experience.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in Arabic and English.
* Strong interpersonal and time-management skills
* Ability to handle multiple tasks and prioritize effectively
* Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information

If you are a motivated and organized individual with a passion for providing administrative support, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity at Grant Thornton Bahrain.
  
  
For further information, and to apply, please visit our website via the “Apply” button below.

data.title: Head of Product
data.raw_snippet:
Head of Product – Regional Growth Leadership

**Bahrain | On-site | Regional Scope (GCC)**

Following a major regional milestone, this fast-scaling digital business is entering its next phase of growth across the GCC. As
**Head of Product**
, you’ll define and deliver the product vision that underpi
data.jd_text:
Head of Product – Regional Growth Leadership

**Bahrain | On-site | Regional Scope (GCC)**

Following a major regional milestone, this fast-scaling digital business is entering its next phase of growth across the GCC. As
**Head of Product**
, you’ll define and deliver the product vision that underpins this expansion.

This is a senior leadership role, based in Bahrain and reporting directly to the CEO, with ownership of product strategy, execution, and commercial outcomes across multiple markets.

The Role:

You’ll lead the product function end-to-end, setting direction while staying close to delivery. The focus is on building and scaling customer-centric digital products that perform at volume, expand into new markets, and deliver measurable commercial results.

Industry-specific domain knowledge can be learned. What matters most is experience operating at scale, leading complex product environments, and driving growth across markets.

This role suits someone who blends strategic thinking with hands-on leadership and has taken products through meaningful scale-up phases.

What You’ll Be Doing:

* Defining and owning the product vision, strategy, and roadmap aligned to regional growth goals
* Leading the full product lifecycle from discovery through delivery and optimisation
* Scaling digital products used by large, growing customer bases
* Driving product-led growth, engagement, and retention across multiple markets
* Working closely with engineering, design, data, and operations to deliver at pace
* Owning product P&L, performance targets, and commercial outcomes
* Leading go-to-market planning for new products and features across the GCC
* Setting and tracking KPIs across product performance, customer impact, and cost efficiency
* Ensuring products operate within regulatory, data, and risk frameworks where required

What You’ll Need to Succeed:

* 10+ years in senior product leadership roles within a scaled digital business
* Proven experience building and scaling products used by large customer bases
* Strong track record owning product P&L or commercial performance
* Experience launching products into new markets or regions
* Comfortable operating in complex, fast-moving environments
* Hands-on leadership style with strong cross-functional influence
* Familiarity with regulated environments is useful, but not essential
* **Bilingual Arabic and English (mandatory)**
* **Willingness to live and work in Bahrain**
  , with regional travel across the GCC

What’s in It for You:

* Executive-level role with direct CEO visibility
* Full ownership of product strategy and outcomes during a key growth phase
* Opportunity to shape products at scale across multiple GCC markets
* Competitive salary
* Join at a pivotal moment and define the next chapter of regional growth

Next Steps:

If you’re exploring senior product leadership roles and want to work at scale in a regional growth environment, get in touch to learn more.

To lead and oversee the organization’s Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) cybersecurity framework by driving the implementation, monitoring, and continuous improvement of cybersecurity initiatives. The role ensures full alignment with Saudi regulatory requirements and internal
data.jd_text:
**Role Purpose:**

To lead and oversee the organization’s Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) cybersecurity framework by driving the implementation, monitoring, and continuous improvement of cybersecurity initiatives. The role ensures full alignment with Saudi regulatory requirements and internal policies while proactively managing cybersecurity risks, strengthening governance practices, and enhancing the organization’s overall security posture.

**Key responsibilities:**

* Lead the development, implementation, and continuous enhancement of the organization’s cybersecurity governance framework in alignment with Saudi cybersecurity regulations and industry best practices.
* Oversee the identification, assessment, and mitigation of cybersecurity risks within the enterprise risk management framework, ensuring effective risk treatment strategies are in place.
* Monitor and enforce compliance with cybersecurity policies, standards, and frameworks, providing regular insights and strategic updates to the Head of GRC and Cybersecurity.
* Manage and coordinate internal and external cybersecurity audits, regulatory assessments, and ensure timely closure of audit findings.
* Conduct and supervise periodic risk assessments, ensuring all gaps are clearly documented, prioritized, and addressed through actionable remediation plans.
* Own and maintain the cybersecurity risk register, ensuring accurate tracking, reporting, and follow-up on mitigation actions.
* Lead the design and delivery of cybersecurity awareness and training programs to enhance organizational security culture.
* Oversee the monitoring of security incidents, ensuring effective coordination with IT and third-party vendors for timely response, escalation, and resolution.
* Supervise third-party cybersecurity risk management activities, ensuring vendors comply with the organization’s security requirements and contractual obligations.
* Prepare and present comprehensive reports on cybersecurity risks, compliance status, KPIs, and performance metrics to senior leadership and board-level committees.
* Stay informed on emerging cybersecurity threats, trends, and regulatory changes, and proactively recommend strategic improvements and controls.
* Provide guidance and mentorship to junior team members, ensuring knowledge sharing and capability development within the team.

**Qualifications:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Information Security, Information Technology, or a related field.
* Preferred certifications: CISA, CISM, ISO 27001 Lead Implementer/Auditor, CRISC, or equivalent.
* Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in both English and Arabic.
* **5–7 years of experience**
  in cybersecurity governance, risk, and compliance, with demonstrated experience in leading initiatives or supervising teams.

data.title: Head of Department of Psychiatry
data.raw_snippet:
**Objective:**
To build on the Department’s strong foundation, fostering an environment of excellence, innovation and continuous enhancement to support the delivery of a high-quality Medicine programme at RCSI Bahrain. Champion academic standards and drive the implementation and continuous improveme
data.jd_text:
**Objective:**
To build on the Department’s strong foundation, fostering an environment of excellence, innovation and continuous enhancement to support the delivery of a high-quality Medicine programme at RCSI Bahrain. Champion academic standards and drive the implementation and continuous improvement of the curriculum, aligned with best practice and ensure the effective delivery of the undergraduate teaching programme in Psychiatry, promoting a culture of collaboration, research engagement and academic development across the School.
  
  
**Reporting To:**
VP of Academic Affairs / Head of School of Medicine
  
  
**PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES**
**Academic Leadership & Governance:**

* Provide strategic academic leadership for the Department of Psychiatry, ensuring excellence in teaching, assessment, research, and clinical integration.
* Be accountable, through established University management and governance structures, for the delivery of all academic commitments associated with the post.
* Lead the design, development, delivery, review, and continuous improvement of undergraduate and postgraduate psychiatry curricula, modules, and assessments.
* Ensure alignment of departmental activities with national and international standards of medical education and training, including Medical Council requirements.
* Contribute to University-wide academic planning, quality assurance, accreditation processes, and academic policy development as required.

**Teaching and Training:**

* Oversee and contribute to clinical and campus-based teaching across junior, intermediate, and senior cycles of the medical programme.
* Ensure the effective delivery of teaching and training at relevant clinical sites, including responsibility for undergraduate learners.
* Provide academic leadership in student assessment, including examination design, standard setting, delivery, evaluation, and feedback processes.
* Support and mentor academic and clinical faculty within the department, fostering excellence in teaching and professional development.
* Ensure appropriate use of the virtual learning environment to support teaching, communication, and student engagement.

**Clinical Leadership and Integration:**

* Be accountable for the clinical component of the post as ensuring integration between academic objectives and clinical service delivery.
* Work collaboratively with Clinical Directors and hospital partners to ensure high-quality psychiatric training and service provision.
* Undertake clinical duties within designated teaching hospitals, with scope and commitment defined in agreement with relevant governance structures.
* Promote best practice in patient care, teaching, and supervision within clinical settings.

**Research and Scholarly Activity:**

* Provide leadership in psychiatric research, supporting departmental research strategy, grant activity, and scholarly output.
* Supervise and mentor faculty, trainees, and students in research activities.
* Contribute to the advancement of psychiatric knowledge through research, publication, and academic collaboration.

**Strategic Development and External Engagement:**

* Play a key role in the development and reform of medical education, training, and research, aligned with Government policy and institutional strategy.
* Collaborate across University and clinical sites, and with postgraduate bodies, regulatory authorities, and professional organisations, on international, national, and regional initiatives.
* Represent the Department and University at internal and external forums as required.

**Compliance & Professional Responsibilities:**

* Ensure adherence to all University, national, and regulatory policies and procedures, including health, safety, and staff welfare requirements.
* Act at all times in the best interests of the Department and University, upholding professional, ethical, and academic standards.
* Undertake such other duties as may reasonably be required in support of the role and the strategic objectives of the University.

Requirements
  
  
**PERSON SPECIFICATION ESSENTIAL:**
  
  
The successful candidate will be a senior academic with substantial leadership experience in clinical education, service delivery, and governance, and will demonstrate the following:
  

* A minimum of 10–15 years post-internship clinical experience in Psychiatry, including senior leadership responsibilities.
* Fellowship and/or Doctoral Degree in Psychiatry from a recognised international awarding body.
* Board Certification in Psychiatry (mandatory).
* Demonstrated experience in academic leadership, including responsibility for programme delivery, curriculum oversight, and faculty supervision.
* Proven track record of undergraduate teaching, assessment design, curriculum development, and quality assurance in a medical education setting.
* Experience of educational innovation, including effective use of e-learning and virtual learning environments (VLEs).
* Evidence of research leadership, including supervision of junior faculty and trainees, scholarly output, and contribution to departmental or institutional research strategy.
* Strong understanding of clinical governance, academic governance, and regulatory standards applicable to medical education and psychiatric training.
* Excellent interpersonal, leadership, and communication skills, with the ability to work collaboratively across academic, clinical, and regulatory stakeholders.
* Demonstrated commitment to the development and reform of medical education, training, and research, aligned with national and international standards

***Note: Appointment will be made at an appropriate level commensurate with experience, qualifications, research track record and institutional requirements.***

data.title: Technical Lead (Rust)
data.raw_snippet:
**About Keyrock**
Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation.
  
  
Today, we rock with over 180 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging
data.jd_text:
**About Keyrock**
Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation.
  
  
Today, we rock with over 180 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Brussels and Singapore, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight.
  
  
We are trading on more than 80 exchanges, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks.
  
  
But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We're pioneers in adopting the Rust development language for our algorithmic trading, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives.
  
  
At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it.
  
  
**Mission statement**
The
**Tech Lead**
plays a pivotal role in bridging
**business objectives**
with
**technical execution**
. This role is not purely managerial nor solely individual contributor, but a
**hybrid position**
that combines
**hands-on engineering**
,
**architectural leadership**
, and
**team enablement**
. A Tech Lead ensures delivery excellence by actively contributing to the codebase, guiding the team technically, and aligning execution with long-term strategic goals.
  
  
At Keyrock, the
**Tech Lead**
is a
**senior technical contributor**
who drives the
**implementation and delivery**
of engineering initiatives within their Business Unit. The Tech Lead is accountable for
**technical execution and delivery quality**
, ensuring that systems are built to high standards of reliability, performance, and maintainability.
  
  
While the
**Head of Engineering (HoE)**
focuses on
**strategic alignment -**
ensuring that engineering outcomes meet the broader objectives agreed with the
**Head of Product (HoP) -**
the Tech Lead operates as the
**executional deputy**
, translating those strategic objectives into code, architecture, and delivery outcomes.
  
  
**Job Description**

* Act as the technical point of accountability for the Service Market Making Business Unit.
* Balance strategic thinking with pragmatic delivery, ensuring systems are built to last while meeting immediate needs.
* Champion collaboration across teams, ensuring alignment between product, design, QA, and engineering.
* Inspire and develop engineers through mentorship, coaching, and technical guidance, fostering growth and autonomy within the team.

Key Responsibilities
  
  

* Drive Delivery and Alignment: Partner with the Head of Engineering to own delivery of entire work-streams, ensuring architecture and engineering decisions align with both immediate requirements and business strategy.
* Balance Code and Leadership: Contribute directly through coding, while also focusing on architecture design, peer reviews, and cross-functional communication.
* Mentor and Elevate Engineers: Provide structured feedback through design and code reviews, support skill development, and empower junior and mid-level engineers to take ownership.
* Technical Leadership: Guide system design, ensure scalability and resilience, and optimise performance across applications and infrastructure.
* Maintain Standards: Uphold engineering best practices, enforce coding guidelines, and partner with QA to embed automation and validation in the delivery pipeline.
* Codebase Health: Manage technical debt, lead refactoring initiatives, and ensure long-term maintainability and operational excellence.
* Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with product managers, designers, and QA engineers to define scope, refine requirements, and plan delivery in a transparent and predictable manner.
* Risk Management: Anticipate technical risks, dependencies, and trade-offs early, and propose mitigations to safeguard delivery timelines.

**Requirements**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience
* 5+ years in a Technical Leadership position guiding teams, shaping architecture, and driving delivery of complex projects
* Demonstrated expertise in architecting and maintaining high-concurrency, low-latency trading systems at scale
* Advanced proficiency in Rust; working knowledge of Node.js (JavaScript/TypeScript) and Python is an advantage
* Deep experience with data-intensive architectures and real-time data pipelines
* Proven track record deploying, optimising and operating AWS environments for ultra-low-latency workloads
* Hands-on experience containerising applications and managing Kubernetes clusters in production
* Familiarity with GitOps workflows to automate infrastructure provisioning, configuration and deployments
* Solid command of software engineering best practices, including Agile methodologies, DevOps principles and end-to-end CI/CD pipelines
* Knowledge of DevSecFinOps principles to embed security, compliance and cost-governance throughout the delivery lifecycle
* Demonstrated leadership of engineering teams, with a commitment to fostering collaboration, mentorship and continuous professional growth
* Excellent communication and stakeholder-management skills, able to partner effectively with Product, Architecture, Quantitative Research and Operations
* Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset, with a passion for building scalable, reliable and secure systems
* Deep domain knowledge of finance and trading, particularly within the cryptocurrency sector
* Experience in fast-paced, dynamic financial or fintech environments is highly desirable

We would like the successful candidate to be based in the UK or Europe. Remote and flexible work options are available.
  
  
Soft Skills & Competencies
  
  

* Ownership & Reliability – Take full responsibility for your work. Deliver high-quality service, meet deadlines, and follow through on commitments. Anticipate and proactively address potential issues before they arise.
* Initiative & Problem-Solving – Approach challenges with a problem-solving entrepreneurial mindset. Make sound, independent judgments and take the initiative to drive solutions rather than waiting for instructions.
* Operational Diligence – Respond strategically to technical and operational challenges, ensuring robust and reliable trading infrastructure while instilling confidence in the team’s processes.
* Curiosity & Growth Mindset – Continuously seek to improve your technical skills and knowledge. Stay ahead of industry trends and take the initiative to practice and explore new technologies that excite you.
* Excellent Communication Skills with a team player mindset, collaborating within a fully remote global team.

We would like the successful candidate to be based in the UK or Europe. Remote and flexible work options are available.
  
  
**Our recruitment philosophy**
We value self-awareness in our recruitment process. We seek people who understand themselves and their career goals. We're after those with the right skills and a conscious choice to join our field. The perfect fit? A crypto enthusiast who’s driven, collaborative, and delivers solid, scalable outcomes.
  
  
**Our offer**

* A competitive salary package, with various benefits depending on the method of engagement (employee or freelancer).
* Autonomy in your time management thanks to flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotely
* The freedom to create your own entrepreneurial experience by being part of a team of people in search of excellence

As an employer we are committed to build an inclusive, diverse and non-discriminating work environment. We welcome employees of all backgrounds, ethnicities, genders, creed and sexual orientation. We hire, reward and promote entirely based on merit and performance.
  
  
Due to the nature of our business and external requirements, we perform background checks on all potential employees, passing which is a prerequisite to join Keyrock.
  
  
https://keyrock.com/careers/

data.title: Head of  BOS Integrated Services Hub 1
data.raw_snippet:
**About This Opportunity**
Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre-sa
data.jd_text:
**About This Opportunity**
Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre-sales and overlooks integrated delivery models setup including System Integration, Application Development & Maintenance , and IT Managed Services.
  
  
This role reports to the Head of SL BOS Delivery Excellence for MEMA and will be part of BOS Delivery Excellence Leadership team.
  
  
What You Will Do:
  
  

* Lead BOS service excellence, managing team of senior services professionals.
* Accountable for services engagement and pre-sales including target topline and profitability.
* Responsible for aligning with Solution Area on Services GTM and drive services offering build up , presales and delivery engagements for the EMEA region.
* Secure service delivery readiness through competence planning inline with business and portfolio evolution.
* Senior People Manager of own unit and a senior Leader across matrixed organization extending to solution line professionals in other CU BOS, BOS solution lines and SD/SDU units.
* Adopt, position and expand integrated service delivery offerings; with focus on customer value realization, delivery efficiency, and adapting to different commercial models.
* Champion delivery efficiency initiatives in collaboration with program management, CU BOS SL and SD/SDU.
* Set Individual Performance and Development goals for members with regular check-ins.
* Responsible for team dimensioning; and Cost Center Responsible to deliver over-Absorption target and required Utilization/Billability.
* Drive a Knowledge Sharing and Asset building culture across their community.
* Instill a culture of add-on sales and customer value maximization.

What You Will Bring:
  
  

* 12+ years of experience in telecom industry with leadership track record in professional services including system integration, application development & maintenance, and IT managed services.
* Deep understanding of BOS portfolio including products and services.
* University degree in ICT/Engineering. A graduate degree such as an MBA is a plus.
* Strong People Manager track record leading Senior Team members and leading Leaders.
* Deep Telecom knowledge including understanding of Telecom networks, OSS & BSS components and familiarity with TmForum (eTOM, SID, TAM)
* Advanced experience with IT Systems and Integration
* OSS and BSS full stack integrations and surrounding ecosystems
* API management & microservice architecture
* Cloud-Native & Platform transformation
* Data analytics, AI/ML applications in service operations
* In depth knowledge of different SDLC methodologies including Agile, Waterfall and DevOps approaches.
* Proven experience with MS IT and ADM related frameworks – ITIL, COBIT & SAFe
* Demonstrated experience leading cross-functional highly matrixed and globally distributed teams.
* Recommended certifications (external): ITIL, Lean Six Sigma, PMP
* Recommended certifications (internal): MSCOO, ICT PD JS8 GIAP certification
* Strong consultative, presentation, and communication skills
* Proven track record to deliver results within a time sensitive and high-pressure environment.
* Highly proficient collaboration skills
* Solid ability to communicate effectively at executive levels.
* In-depth knowledge of running Organizational and Solution Level Governance.
* Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.

Note: There are a total of 4 Head of Integration Services Hub vacancies with the same responsibility and skills requirements.
  
  
Ericsson South Africa recruits in line with its Employment Equity plan and will therefore give preference to suitably qualified candidates who are members of designated groups. Would you like to take a tour to “Life at Ericsson MEA”, visit short video Click Here
  
  
**Why join Ericsson?**
At Ericsson, you´ll have an outstanding opportunity. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next.
  
  
**What happens once you apply?**
Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like.
  
  
Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more.
  
  
**Req ID:**
776664

data.title: Territory Sales Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**TERRITORY LOCATION: EASTERN US**

As Territory Sales Manager you will grow CERATIZIT’s Industrial Wear Parts and Wood & Stone working business across North America. You will manage and expand an existing customer base while developing new accounts through consultative, solution-oriented selling. T
data.jd_text:
**TERRITORY LOCATION: EASTERN US**

As Territory Sales Manager you will grow CERATIZIT’s Industrial Wear Parts and Wood & Stone working business across North America. You will manage and expand an existing customer base while developing new accounts through consultative, solution-oriented selling. This is a high-visibility, field-based role with significant travel and the autonomy to run your territory like a business. It’s an excellent opportunity to build enterprise selling and stakeholder-management skills—owning customers end-to-end while learning to drive cross-functional execution in a global organization. Success requires comfort working remotely in the US while influencing a global, matrix organization—driving timely quotations, realistic lead times, and clear follow-up with production, customer service, and segment management teams in Europe/Asia to deliver on customer commitments.

**RESPONSIBILITIES/DUTIES:**

* Build new business in your territory by identifying and developing opportunities, proactively prospecting, and partnering with the Regional Sales Director on account strategy.
* Act as a trusted technical and commercial resource for customers across the Hard Material Solutions portfolio (Wood & Stone working and Industrial Wear Parts).
* Manage commercial risk by flagging customer financial issues early, partnering with customer service, and supporting credit control follow-up as needed.
* Support strategic projects by coordinating input from the North American sales team and presenting recommendations to the Regional Sales Director.
* Maintain forecasting for your territory which includes building quarterly/annual budgets, review forecasts regularly, and maintain an up-to-date pipeline with clear close plans.

**EDUCATION AND/OR EXPERIENCE REQUIRED:**

* Proven skills in sales and new business development. Bachelor’s degree in business is preferred, or 3 years of experience in industrial wear parts.
* Strong mechanical aptitude. Prior experience in the carbide industry is highly preferred.
* Proven ability to conduct training, and good presentation skills to both large and small groups
* Ability to work independently from a home office
* Ability to travel in the assigned territory
* Computer literacy with Microsoft Office and CRM systems (SIP)
* Good communication skills

**TRAVEL REQUIREMENTS:**

* 50% to 60% travel both nationally and abroad / overnight stays required
