data.title: Project's Manager – Interior Fit Out
data.raw_snippet:
Benchmark Fit out is a leading Fit out company looking for a Projects Manager to handle the projects.

* **Responsibilities**

As Fitout Projects Manager is responsible for :

• Managing and overseeing complex fitout projects from start to finish. In addition to the responsibilities of a Fitout Proj
data.jd_text:
Benchmark Fit out is a leading Fit out company looking for a Projects Manager to handle the projects.

* **Responsibilities**

As Fitout Projects Manager is responsible for :

• Managing and overseeing complex fitout projects from start to finish. In addition to the responsibilities of a Fitout Project Manager.

• Mentoring and leading project teams: As a senior manager, the Fitout Project Manager may be responsible for mentoring and leading junior project managers and project teams to ensure that projects are delivered successfully.

• Developing and implementing project management processes: The Senior Fitout Project Manager may be responsible for developing and implementing project management processes and best practices to ensure that projects are managed effectively and efficiently.

• Developing and maintaining relationships with clients and stakeholders: The Senior Fitout Project Manager must be able to develop and maintain strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and other professionals in the industry to ensure that projects are delivered to their satisfaction.

• Identifying and managing complex project risks: The Senior Fitout Project Manager must have the ability to identify and manage complex project risks and to implement contingency plans to minimize their impact on the project.

• Ensuring compliance with industry standards and regulations: The Senior Fitout Project Manager must ensure that all projects are completed in compliance with industry standards and regulations, and that all safety, quality, and environmental considerations are taken into account.

• In summary, a Senior Fitout Project Manager is a highly experienced and skilled professional who has a deep understanding of the design and construction process.

They are responsible for managing complex fitout projects and leading project teams to deliver projects successfully. They must have excellent project management skills, strong leadership abilities, and the ability to build and maintain relationships with clients and stakeholders

Qualifications

• Bachelor's Degree of Architectural or equivalent experience

• 10 year experience on similar role

• Strong verbal, written, and organizational skills

data.title: Technical Project Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**Drive Delivery. Create Clarity. Keep Teams Moving Forward.**

Our IT team is looking for a Technical Project Manager who thrives at the intersection of product, engineering, and delivery. This role is critical to ensuring complex technical initiatives move from idea to execution with clarity, pred
data.jd_text:
**Drive Delivery. Create Clarity. Keep Teams Moving Forward.**

Our IT team is looking for a Technical Project Manager who thrives at the intersection of product, engineering, and delivery. This role is critical to ensuring complex technical initiatives move from idea to execution with clarity, predictability, and momentum.

You will work closely with Product Managers, Engineering Managers, and Tech Leads to translate requirements into actionable plans, keep delivery on track, and remove obstacles before they become risks. If you are energized by ownership, execution, and helping teams deliver consistently in fast-moving environments, this role is for you.

**What You’ll Be Doing:**

**Delivery Planning & Execution**

* Partner with Product Managers and Engineering Managers to translate BRDs and requirements into clear, executable delivery plans.
* Define and maintain realistic timelines, milestones, and schedules across multiple teams and systems.
* Track day-to-day progress, ensuring tasks move forward and following up with owners to prevent delays.
* Own delivery accountability by clearly answering:
  *Are we on track, and if not, what are we doing about it?*

**Cross-Team Coordination & Risk Management**

* Manage dependencies across frontend, backend, ERP, integrations, infrastructure, and supporting systems.
* Identify, track, and escalate risks, blockers, and delivery threats early, while driving mitigation options.
* Support Engineering Managers in protecting team focus and managing scope changes during sprints and delivery cycles.
* Monitor work-in-progress, bottlenecks, and context switching to improve delivery flow and predictability.

**Agile Delivery & Process Excellence**

* Plan and facilitate core agile ceremonies including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, reviews/demos, and retrospectives.
* Ensure Jira boards remain accurate, transparent, and reliable, with clearly defined ownership and deliverable work items.
* Break down work into testable, incremental deliverables that support smooth development and QA cycles.
* Help teams improve estimation, planning discipline, and delivery predictability over time.
* Drive continuous improvement across the SDLC by identifying friction points and implementing practical, value-driven process enhancements.

**Stakeholder Communication**

* Provide concise, structured status updates to stakeholders, highlighting progress, risks, decisions needed, and timeline impact.
* Ensure leadership has clear visibility into delivery health without unnecessary noise or ambiguity.

**What You Bring to the Table:**

* 2–5 years of experience as a
  Technical Project Manager, Delivery Manager, or similar role in a technology-driven environment.
* Strong understanding of software development lifecycles and Agile delivery practices.
* Experience working with cross-functional technical teams across multiple systems and platforms.
* Hands-on experience using Jira (or similar tools) to manage delivery and execution.
* Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple streams of work simultaneously.
* Clear, confident communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
* A proactive mindset focused on problem-solving, accountability, and continuous improvement.

data.title: Project Manager - Construction (Remediation)
data.raw_snippet:
**Building on our past. Ready for the future**
Worley is a global professional services company of energy, chemicals and resources experts. We partner with customers to deliver projects and create value over the life of their assets. We’re bridging two worlds, moving towards more sustainable energy
data.jd_text:
**Building on our past. Ready for the future**
Worley is a global professional services company of energy, chemicals and resources experts. We partner with customers to deliver projects and create value over the life of their assets. We’re bridging two worlds, moving towards more sustainable energy sources, while helping to provide the energy, chemicals and resources needed now.
  
  
**About The Job**
We’re looking for a Leader, Construction / Construction Project Manager to join our Ahmadi, Kuwait team. Be a part of Worley's dynamic team as we extend our management of the KERP program for another five years. With million m2 of land successfully cleared from contamination and million tons remediated, we're leading the charge in sustainable environmental restoration. Join us and contribute to this incredible journey of transforming contaminated sites into thriving ecosystems.
  
  
When you work for us, you get the chance to join nearly 50,000 people all driven by a common purpose – delivering a more sustainable world. We’re excited about the challenges ahead. If you ask us, there’s never been a more rewarding time to work in the energy, chemical and resources sectors.
  
  
**What You'll Do**

* Management, control, coordination and supervision services as well as assistance for the assigned project inclusive of budget formulation, cost control and quality assurance.
* Management of support services for supervising and directing engineering, procurement and management of construction contractors appointed by the Client.
* Management of services for Oil & Gas production activities including start-up and initial operations assistance as well as operational personnel and systems as required by the Client.
* Reports to the Line Manager and is authorized to deputize for him during his absence with a proven track record of managing multi-projects in general and Design & Engineering and Construction function in particular, in the oil and gas industry
* Is responsible for developing the overall strategy for soil remediation in the assigned areas, it includes contracting strategies and management of contractors.
* Manage and coordinate multidiscipline engineering activities to ensure projects such as FEED (front end engineering design) studies and preparation, ITB (invitation to bid) check estimate preparation, LSTK (lump sum turnkey) tender package preparation are executed in a safe, efficient, cost effective and timely manner.
* Act as primary technical coordination interface with client to ensure that the client’s expectations are satisfied.
* Ensure the project conforms to the client’s approved Basis of Design and all applicable regulatory and code requirements.
* We’re looking for a Leader, Construction / Construction Project Manager to join our Ahmadi, Kuwait team. Be a part of Worley's dynamic team as we extend our management of the KERP program for another five years. With million m2 of land successfully cleared from contamination and million tons remediated, we're leading the charge in sustainable environmental restoration. Join us and contribute to this incredible journey of transforming contaminated sites into thriving ecosystems.
* When you work for us, you get the chance to join nearly 50,000 people all driven by a common purpose – delivering a more sustainable world. We’re excited about the challenges ahead. If you ask us, there’s never been a more rewarding time to work in the energy, chemical and resources sector

**What You'll Have**
**HSE Management**

* Maintains HSE focus within the team to ensure OHSE requirements included in design and procurement.

**Client Relationships**

* Maintains and builds healthy client relationships.
* Proactively resolves performance, scope and schedule issues with the Client as they arises
* Identifies, documents, communicates and meets the client’s requirements

**Scope Management**

* Compliance with WPMP (Worley Project Management Plan)
* Compliance with requirements of the contract.
* Effective change control process application.
* Achievement of claims mitigation and claims management if appropriate.

**Cost Control**

* Monitors cost aspects of the project within direct control to forecast final costs, identifies trends and takes action to avoid cost overruns.
* Promotes implementation of VIP (value improving practices) on the assigned project to achieve overall effectiveness of the use of capital on projects.

**Quality Management**

* Contributes to compliance with Worley policies and procedures.
* Strong working knowledge of Worley engineering and procurement systems and procedures.

**Team Management**

* Focuses team on achievement of milestones and other key project objectives.
* Listens to the team, discusses the issues, resolves problems and directs activities to remove roadblocks and achieve objectives.

**Design Management**

* Sets clear objectives to the design team, including achieving productivity and budget targets.
* Ensures design team aware of, and complies with, applicable regulations and codes.
* Manages inter-discipline interfaces effectively.

**About You**
You will have Bachelor's degree in Engineering with a remarkable year of experience.
  
  

* Remarkable year of experience.
* You are an individual who is self-motivated, proactive, and works well with a team.
* You will be seen as the Subject matter specialist for the resolution of issues arising across all areas of the job and be able to clearly explain complex processes that surround the role.
* We expect you to ensure the integrity of the work scope and that all work is carried-out in accordance with applicable codes and standards.
* We expect you to mentor of less experienced members of staff including ‘hands on’ approach to the formulation and delivery of training programs for local Kuwaiti personnel.

**Why you should apply**

* Providing an agile culture and challenging work opportunities results in a positive work environment. Our people enjoy competitive compensation packages and a fun, personal, collaborative and safe working environment. We value equal employment opportunity and are committed to promoting fairness, equality and diversity.
* Our policy is to conduct background checks for all candidates who accept an offer of employment with us.

**Moving forward together**
We’re committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels they belong, can bring themselves, and are heard. We provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants and employees without regard to age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, disability status, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, citizenship status or any other basis as protected by law.
  
  
We want our people to be energized and empowered to drive sustainable impact. So, our focus is on a values-inspired culture that unlocks brilliance through belonging, connection and innovation.
  
  
And we're not just talking about it; we're doing it. We're reskilling our people, leveraging transferable skills, and supporting the transition of our workforce to become experts in today's low carbon energy infrastructure and technology.
  
  
Whatever your ambition, there’s a path for you here. And there’s no barrier to your potential career success. Join us to broaden your horizons, explore diverse opportunities, and be part of delivering sustainable change.

* Ensure work is executed as per approved drawings, MEW standards, and safety requirements.
* Coordinate with site engineers, subcontractors, and workers.
* Monitor work progress and report to the Project Manager / Site Engineer.

data.title: Chef De Partie
data.raw_snippet:
**Key Responsibilities:**

* Directs, coaches, supports, supervises, and evaluates (with the Sous Chef) all direct reports.
* Must have excellent food knowledge and a full understanding of culinary terms.
* Must be able to read, understand, follow, and prepare company recipes by maintaining the qual
data.jd_text:
**Key Responsibilities:**

* Directs, coaches, supports, supervises, and evaluates (with the Sous Chef) all direct reports.
* Must have excellent food knowledge and a full understanding of culinary terms.
* Must be able to read, understand, follow, and prepare company recipes by maintaining the quality and consistency in taste according to the instructions provided by the corporate office.
* Must be proficient in completing tasks, work efficiently and productively.
* Must be able to work in any section of a kitchen.
* Coordinate and supervise all team members assigned to their section, assign and delegate tasks accordingly.
* Undertake recipe reviews daily. Maintain recipe folders in immaculate condition.
* Responsible for follow-through on any request within the area of responsibility received from the direct supervisor or manager on duty; this may include show plates, food samples and random food tastings.
* Control production levels and recommend ideas for improvements and better cost controlling.
* Prepare daily electronic food requisitions needed for his section production and countercheck deliveries for its accuracy; Report any discrepancies to his immediate supervisor.
* Must be able to oversee any main section (Sauce, Entremetier, Fish, Roast, Outlet CDP, Tournant and Cold Kitchen) and its entire food production.
* Maintain and ensure that Public Health are followed according to company standards and expectation (referring to US, Anvisa, Shipsan, Canadian, Australian etc.) and always followed within their section.
* Ensures that the HACCP program is carried out correctly.
* Maintains their assigned area in preparation for announced or unannounced United States Public Health type inspection either done by the Food & Beverage Director or the actual inspectors.
* Reports for duty at assigned times, follows their supervisor’s instructions, and ensures that personal appearance, uniform, and personal hygiene are in accordance with the company’s rules and regulations.

**Qualifications and Skills**

* A minimum of 8 years in an upscale hotel, resort, cruise ship or convention banqueting service and at least 2 years as Chef de Partie (shipboard experience preferred).
* A culinary school degree is required.
* Very strong management skills in a multicultural and dynamic environment.
* Very strong communication, problem solving, decision making, and interpersonal skills.
* Superior customer service, teambuilding, and conflict resolution skills.
* Knowledge of the principles and processes for providing personalized services including needs assessment techniques, quality service standards, alternative delivery systems, and guest satisfaction evaluation techniques.
* Strong planning, coaching, organizing, staffing, controlling, and evaluating skills.
* Intermediate computer software skills required.
* Possess a good understanding of basic accounting principles such as numbering flow, “Debits/Credits”, adjusting entries, and corrections.
* Ability to write reports and business correspondence and to establish a good rapport with the ship’s Senior Officers and the corporate office.

**Description**
Seeking urgently to hire an Accounts & Finance Manager who will oversee daily accounting operations, financial reporting, budgeting, and cash flow management. Must fulfill the below criteria:
  
  
Key Responsibilities & Duties
  
  

* Financial Operations: Manage daily accounting functions, including accounts payable/receivable, payroll, and cash flow to ensure accurate financial records.
* Reporting & Analysis: Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements, MIS reports, and forecasts to analyze business performance.
* Budgeting & Planning: Develop, implement, and maintain budgetary control procedures and strategic, long-term business plans.
* Compliance & Auditing: Ensure compliance with local laws and manage internal/external audit processes.

data.title: Senior Accountant
data.raw_snippet:
**Company Description**

Benchmark Fitout is a full-service construction and fit-out firm, specializing in delivering high-quality projects from initial concept to completion. The company supports clients through all project phases, including planning, design, obtaining permissions and approvals, co
data.jd_text:
**Company Description**

Benchmark Fitout is a full-service construction and fit-out firm, specializing in delivering high-quality projects from initial concept to completion. The company supports clients through all project phases, including planning, design, obtaining permissions and approvals, construction, after-sales support, and maintenance. Benchmark Fitout is dedicated to providing tailored solutions and ensuring customer satisfaction. With a focus on excellence, the firm has established itself as a trusted partner for high-end fit-out services.

Job Title: Senior Accountant (Fit-Out / Contracting)

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Accountant to oversee financial operations within a fit-out contracting environment. The candidate will be responsible for managing project-based accounting, cost control, financial reporting, and ensuring compliance with company policies and local regulations.

⸻

Key Responsibilities

1. Financial Management & Reporting

• Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements

• Monitor cash flow, budgeting, and financial forecasting

• Maintain general ledger and ensure accuracy of all financial transactions

• Prepare management reports and financial analysis for decision-making

2. Project Accounting (Critical for Fit-Out)

• Track project costs against budgets (materials, labor, subcontractors)

• Monitor project profitability and cost deviations

• Coordinate with project managers for cost allocation and reporting

• Review BOQs, variations (VOs), and project billing

3. Accounts Payable & Receivable

• Manage supplier payments, subcontractor invoices, and reconciliations

• Follow up on client payments and maintain strong cash flow

• Review contracts and payment terms to ensure compliance

4. Cost Control & Budgeting

• Implement cost control procedures across all projects

• Analyze variances between actual vs. budgeted costs

• Support procurement and commercial teams in cost evaluation

5. Compliance & Audit

• Ensure compliance with local tax regulations and financial policies

• Coordinate with external auditors and prepare audit schedules

• Maintain proper documentation for all financial transactions

6. Payroll & HR Coordination

• Oversee payroll processing in coordination with HR

• Ensure compliance with labor laws and employee benefits

⸻

Qualifications

• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

• Professional certification (CPA / ACCA / CMA) is preferred

• Minimum 5–8 years of experience, with at least 3 years in contracting or fit-out industry

. should be based on Kuwait & transfer visa from day 1z

⸻

Skills & Competencies

• Strong knowledge of project accounting & cost control

• Experience in construction/fit-out financial cycles

• Proficiency in accounting software (ERP systems preferred)

• Advanced Excel skills

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

• High attention to detail and accuracy

• Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines

⸻

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

• Accuracy and timeliness of financial reports

• Project cost variance control

• Cash flow management efficiency

• Collection period (receivables)

• Compliance with audit and regulatory requirements

⸻

Preferred Experience

• Experience working with fit-out companies, construction firms, or interior contracting

• Familiarity with BOQ, progress billing, retention, and variation orders

• Experience in Kuwait market

Reporting Line

Reports directly to: Finance Manager / Managing Director

⸻

Optional (You can include this if you want it stronger for hiring)

Personality Profile:

• Commercially aware (not just accounting)

• Strong coordination with site and project teams

• Able to challenge costs and protect company profitability

data.title: Software Engineering Director
data.raw_snippet:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
  
  
Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together u
data.jd_text:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
  
  
Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together under a single director of engineering. Once you have passed the first round of interviews we will discuss specific software products which would be a good fit for your interests.
  
  
We believe that open source is just starting to transform the tech sector and enterprise compute. Our goal is to make open source easier, more reliable and more secure for deployment and development. We strive to be the provider of 'most software to most companies'. To deliver on that ambition, our engineers are carefully selected from the applicants across the globe. We select for brilliance and motivation to take open source to the next level. Our engineering directors set the pace and the direction to help teams achieve more than they realised they could, and feel proud of the result.
  
  
Even though this is a senior management position, managing managers, we expect Engineering Directors to be outstanding developers with the confidence to lead by example across the full range of activities in their teams. You need to understand what great code looks like in the language and genre of your product - whether that is a web service, or a system daemon, or a desktop application, or a distributed system. You'll need to know how to drive testing and benchmarking to improve the clarity in your teams around quality and performance. You'll also need to be confident to set and defend high expectations of work ethic, consistency, focus and learning.
  
  
We have director level roles across a wide range of engineering domains, including:
  
  

* Python and Golang
* C / C++ / Rust
* Data infrastructure
* HTML / CSS / JavaScript / Typescript / React
* Flutter
* Distro packaging and systems
* SAAS and web microservices
* Kernel
* Servers
* Graphics, Browser and Desktop
* Silicon enablement and embedded devices
* Product Security

If your domain of expertise isn't listed above, yet you feel it's relevant to Canonical, then feel free to apply anyway. We will route you to the most suitable team.
  
  
Location: The role is remote, with positions available in all time zones. It includes a requirement for global travel for twice per year, for trips up to two weeks long.
  
  
What you'll do
  
  

* Lead multiple teams of engineers, ranging from graduate to senior
* Develop your engineering managers and maintain Canonical culture
* Hire staff level engineers for specific roles like performance and quality
* Coach, mentor, and offer career development feedback
* Identify and measure indicators of team health and productivity
* Ensure rounded delivery including community participation and docs
* Implement disciplined engineering processes and defend them
* Set and measure progress against benchmark goals for speed and efficiency
* Represent your product to stakeholders, partners, and customers
* Develop and evangelise great engineering and organisational practices
* Plan and manage progress on agreed goals and projects
* Be an active part of the leadership team, collaborating with other leaders
* Collaborate with leads for related or dependent products

What we're looking for in you
  
  

* An exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
* Undergraduate degree in Computer Science or STEM, or a compelling narrative about your alternative path
* Extensive management experience and confidence to set high expectations
* Outstanding Linux based software engineering track record
* Drive and a track record of going above-and-beyond expectations
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
* Public speaking and presentation skills
* A love of developing and growing people and a track record of it
* Experience in leading, coaching and mentoring software developers
* Organised and able to ensure your team delivers timely, high quality results
* Well-organised, self-starting and able to deliver to schedule
* Professional manner interacting with colleagues, partners, and community
* You have advanced expertise in your own domain
* You are knowledgeable and passionate about software development
* You have solid experience working in an agile development environment
* You have a demonstrated drive for continual learning
* Builds trust, relationships and confidence
* Result-oriented, with a personal drive to meet commitments
* Ability to travel twice a year, for company events up to two weeks each

**Additional Skills We Value**

* Experience in a developer advocacy or community role
* Open source community engagement and leadership
* Ops and system administration experience
* Performance engineering and security experience

What we offer you
  
  
We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
  
  

* Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events

**About Canonical**
Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Canonical has been a remote-first company since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
  
  
Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
  
  
We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

data.jd_text:
**About This Opportunity**
Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre-sales and overlooks integrated delivery models setup including System Integration, Application Development & Maintenance , and IT Managed Services.
  
  
This role reports to the Head of SL BOS Delivery Excellence for MEMA and will be part of BOS Delivery Excellence Leadership team.
  
  
What You Will Do:
  
  

* Lead BOS service excellence, managing team of senior services professionals.
* Accountable for services engagement and pre-sales including target topline and profitability.
* Responsible for aligning with Solution Area on Services GTM and drive services offering build up , presales and delivery engagements for the EMEA region.
* Secure service delivery readiness through competence planning inline with business and portfolio evolution.
* Senior People Manager of own unit and a senior Leader across matrixed organization extending to solution line professionals in other CU BOS, BOS solution lines and SD/SDU units.
* Adopt, position and expand integrated service delivery offerings; with focus on customer value realization, delivery efficiency, and adapting to different commercial models.
* Champion delivery efficiency initiatives in collaboration with program management, CU BOS SL and SD/SDU.
* Set Individual Performance and Development goals for members with regular check-ins.
* Responsible for team dimensioning; and Cost Center Responsible to deliver over-Absorption target and required Utilization/Billability.
* Drive a Knowledge Sharing and Asset building culture across their community.
* Instill a culture of add-on sales and customer value maximization.

What You Will Bring:
  
  

* 12+ years of experience in telecom industry with leadership track record in professional services including system integration, application development & maintenance, and IT managed services.
* Deep understanding of BOS portfolio including products and services.
* University degree in ICT/Engineering. A graduate degree such as an MBA is a plus.
* Strong People Manager track record leading Senior Team members and leading Leaders.
* Deep Telecom knowledge including understanding of Telecom networks, OSS & BSS components and familiarity with TmForum (eTOM, SID, TAM)
* Advanced experience with IT Systems and Integration
* OSS and BSS full stack integrations and surrounding ecosystems
* API management & microservice architecture
* Cloud-Native & Platform transformation
* Data analytics, AI/ML applications in service operations
* In depth knowledge of different SDLC methodologies including Agile, Waterfall and DevOps approaches.
* Proven experience with MS IT and ADM related frameworks – ITIL, COBIT & SAFe
* Demonstrated experience leading cross-functional highly matrixed and globally distributed teams.
* Recommended certifications (external): ITIL, Lean Six Sigma, PMP
* Recommended certifications (internal): MSCOO, ICT PD JS8 GIAP certification
* Strong consultative, presentation, and communication skills
* Proven track record to deliver results within a time sensitive and high-pressure environment.
* Highly proficient collaboration skills
* Solid ability to communicate effectively at executive levels.
* In-depth knowledge of running Organizational and Solution Level Governance.
* Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.

Note: There are a total of 4 Head of Integration Services Hub vacancies with the same responsibility and skills requirements.
  
  
Ericsson South Africa recruits in line with its Employment Equity plan and will therefore give preference to suitably qualified candidates who are members of designated groups. Would you like to take a tour to “Life at Ericsson MEA”, visit short video Click Here
  
  
**Why join Ericsson?**
At Ericsson, you´ll have an outstanding opportunity. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next.
  
  
**What happens once you apply?**
Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like.
  
  
Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more.

data.title: PMO Governance & Assurance Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**SUMMARY OF ROLE**

The Assurance & Governance Manager – PMO is responsible for ensuring robust governance, control, and assurance across the Bank’s project and program portfolio. The role oversees adherence to the project management framework, policies, and procedures, ensuring consistent, high-qu
data.jd_text:
**SUMMARY OF ROLE**

The Assurance & Governance Manager – PMO is responsible for ensuring robust governance, control, and assurance across the Bank’s project and program portfolio. The role oversees adherence to the project management framework, policies, and procedures, ensuring consistent, high-quality delivery aligned with time, cost, and quality objectives. The role also drives PMO maturity by conducting health checks, enhancing governance standards, strengthening reporting accuracy, and embedding continuous improvement practices across all projects and programs within the portfolio.

**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Portfolio Governance & Reporting**

* Lead the preparation and consolidation of weekly and monthly portfolio reports, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and alignment with actual project performance.
* Review and challenge inputs from Project Managers to ensure quality, consistency, and transparency of reporting across all governance forums.
* Ensure all reporting is delivered within agreed timelines and meets senior management and governance requirements.

**Project Assurance & Health Checks**

* Conduct periodic project health checks across all programs and projects to assess performance against scope, timeline, cost, and quality.
* Identify gaps, risks, and non-compliance areas, and ensure timely tracking and closure of corrective actions.
* Provide independent assurance on project delivery and escalate critical issues impacting portfolio performance.

**Risk, Issue & Dependency Management**

* Monitor and ensure effective management of risks, issues, and interdependencies across projects.
* Highlight and escalate gaps where risks and issues are not actively managed by project teams.
* Support mitigation planning to minimize impact on project delivery and overall portfolio performance.

**PMO Controls & Registers Management**

* Oversee and maintain PMO registers, including risk logs, change control, contracts, VAPT exceptions, and lessons learned.
* Ensure registers are consistently updated, accurate, and aligned with governance requirements.
* Implement standardized processes for tracking and reporting across all PMO control areas.

**Governance Forums & Stakeholder Coordination**

* Plan, coordinate, and facilitate governance forums including project boards and steering committees.
* Ensure accurate documentation of meeting minutes, decisions, and actions within defined timelines.
* Track and follow up on all actions to ensure timely closure and accountability.

**Financial Governance & Budget Oversight**

* Establish and monitor project budget tracking and forecasting processes aligned with approved plans.
* Ensure financial data is accurately reflected in portfolio reporting and governance discussions.

**Post-Implementation Review & Continuous Improvement**

* Review and quality assure post-implementation reviews to ensure alignment between planned and actual outcomes.
* Ensure lessons learned are captured, documented, and shared across the PMO.
* Conduct quarterly analysis of lessons learned to identify trends and drive continuous improvement

**Process Improvement & PMO Maturity**

* Identify opportunities to enhance PMO processes, tools, and governance frameworks.
* Lead initiatives to improve PMO maturity, efficiency, and effectiveness.
* Promote best practices in project management and governance across the organization.

**Stakeholder Management & Collaboration**

* Build and maintain strong working relationships with project sponsors, business units, IT, and senior stakeholders.
* Influence stakeholders to ensure adherence to governance standards and successful project delivery.
* Provide guidance and support to Project Managers on governance, reporting, and assurance requirements.

Requirements
  
  

· Omani National

· Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Project Management, or a related field

· Minimum 5–7 years of experience in project management, PMO, or governance roles

data.jd_text:
**About the job**

To provide top-level subject matter expertise on critical steam and gas turbines, lead and manage strategic projects, overseeing all aspects of operational, financial, customer, and employee strategy throughout the project's lifecycle. The role ensures that projects are meticulously planned, effectively managed, and financially controlled. It includes coordinating with stakeholders, ensuring clear communication, evaluating progress, implementing necessary changes, and delivering timely reports in alignment with the evolving needs of the project. This position requires a strategic mindset to drive success across all project dimensions and ensure alignment with broader

* Responsible for the P&L, budget, and commercial oversight of projects, including managing cash flow, collections, and delivering budgets aligned with forecasts and expectations.
* Perform Collaborate on the techno-commercial aspects of strategic projects, ensuring technical feasibility, cost efficiency, and commercial viability across the project lifecycle.
* Lead the planning, execution, and quality control of machining and maintenance activities for all rotating equipment, optimizing processes for efficiency and performance.
* Ensure adherence to company policies, with a strong focus on managing operations in line with safety regulations and company safety rules.
* Drive project processes and procedures to achieve high-quality, first-time delivery while meeting customer satisfaction goals
* Take up Lead Field Service Engineer duties to support Gas Turbine (GT), Steam Turbine (ST), and Generator job activities. This includes planning and coordinating maintenance activities, overseeing documentation processes, and leading troubleshooting efforts.
* Provide technical expertise and assistance for Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (MHI) units as required. This includes offering specialized knowledge on the design, operation, and troubleshooting of MHI equipment to ensure smooth functioning and addressing technical challenges promptly.

We are seeking an experienced
**Project Manager**
to join our team in Muscat. This is a full-time, on-site role responsible for overseeing the planning, execution, and delivery of IT and digital transformation projects.

The Project Manager will coordinate project resources, manage project timelines, and ensure successful delivery within scope and budget. The role also involves collaborating with cross-functional teams, monitoring progress, and ensuring quality standards are met throughout the project lifecycle.

Key Responsibilities

* Plan, manage, and deliver IT and digital transformation projects
* Coordinate project resources and cross-functional teams
* Monitor project progress and ensure timely delivery
* Manage project risks, issues, and dependencies
* Ensure projects remain within scope, timeline, and budget
* Conduct project meetings and provide regular status updates
* Ensure quality control and compliance with project requirements
* Collaborate with stakeholders and technical teams
* Maintain project documentation and governance

Qualifications & Experience

* Minimum
  **8 years of experience**
  in IT and digital transformation project management
* Proven experience managing
  **large-scale IT projects**
* **Experience working with government or enterprise environments**
  is preferred
* Experience managing
  **cross-functional teams**
* **Strong organizational and time management skills**
* **Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills**
* **Understanding of IT-related technologies and solutions**

Education

* Bachelor's degree in Project Management, Information Technology, Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or equivalent

Certifications (Preferred)

* PMP (Project Management Professional)
* PRINCE2 Certification
* Agile or Scrum Certifications

data.title: Executive Director – Head of GTx Oman
data.raw_snippet:
**JOB PURPOSE:**

The role is primarily responsible for executing the GTx strategy of Oman, working with FAB CEO in Oman and FAB Head of coverage Oman in leading developments and sales, structuring GTx solutions, managing and growing of trade and cash business for FAB across Oman local clients in-co
data.jd_text:
**JOB PURPOSE:**

The role is primarily responsible for executing the GTx strategy of Oman, working with FAB CEO in Oman and FAB Head of coverage Oman in leading developments and sales, structuring GTx solutions, managing and growing of trade and cash business for FAB across Oman local clients in-country, outbound and inbound business originating from our network.

* The job is critical in channeling and executing GTx HO strategy into Oman.
* The role is based in Muscat (Oman)

**KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES:**

* Setting and executing the strategy for the business, assess the market opportunities and challenges, and overall parameters of the business, in line with Head of GTx GCC ex UAE and Head of GTx International

**Job Context:**

**Specific Accountability**

As Executive Director Head of GTx Oman, you will be responsible for:

* Actively engage with clients together with the Head of coverage, Oman and Oman Coverage team to build GTx business for the Bank in the country. A strong drive for GTx Sales, understanding of the GTx Products and clients in Oman.

* Delivery of specific revenue targets returns on risk adjusted assets and equity consistent with the Bank’s acceptable risk parameters. Keeping the management aware of performance gaps with suggestions to rectify and improve performance and deepen client relationship

* Structuring, executing and implementing workable and innovative solutions that meet customer needs. Providing technical expertise to relationship managers and facilitating client discussions. Lead structuring of bespoke GTx Solutions and ensure high-quality customer experience through consultative approach

* Establishing strong stakeholder relationships and work with colleagues and partners across the network to build a strong GTx Franchise in Oman. Work closely with Cash Management and Trade Finance Product Management team in UAE to finalize related documentation/ structures/ rollout new products/ solutions/policies. Provide Oman country / competition feedback to review Bank’s product offering in Oman and potential up-tiering of the product suite.

* Working closely with Coverage teams in-country and network to develop markets, clients, and products based on detailed Client Account Plan for growing GTx business and maximizing market share. Partnering with coverage teams to develop quality credit proposals supporting structuring of existing and new facilities

* Ensuring transactions, facilities and implementation are being executed in a timely manner. Working closely with Compliance, Finance, Implementation, Operations, etc. for client on-boarding and smooth execution of transactions

* Identifying and managing fraud, regulatory and compliance risks in the transactions when developing solutions for clients. Proactively managing transactional risk in an efficient and profitable manner.

* Identifying opportunities for continuous improvement of products, systems, processes, and practices while considering client needs, market developments and trends. Actively participate in opportunities to update product skills, understand market trends and competitor behaviors

* Understanding and complying with regulations and internal policies i.e. Credit/ Compliance/ Operational Risks, etc.

**FRAMEWORKS, BOUNDARIES, & DECISION-MAKING AUTHORITY:**

* Strong Client network with focus to grow business for the bank in the country. Strong origination capability coupled with excellent customer service & negotiation skills
* Strong technical knowledge in GTx products including Cash Management, Channels and Trade Finance
* Self-starter who can handle all enquiries independently and able to effectively interact with customer to resolve enquiries.
* Strong interpersonal skills as well as a team player with strong influential skill that can motivate other team members to achieve the business objective. Ability to work in Matrix environment
* Self-confident and performance driven. Ability to work under pressure and with a strong delivery focus.
* Ability to articulate, formulate and deliver high quality client presentation
* Understanding of operations processes and procedures, and related risk in trade transactions
* Familiar with the regulatory environment concerning financial industry in country
* Strong written and spoken English (Arabic is a plus)

**QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE:**

**Minimum Qualification:**
Degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Commerce or equivalent discipline

**Minimum Experience: 15+**
years Transaction Banking experience.

**Omani National**

data.title: External Affairs & Partnerships Manager - (Oman)
data.raw_snippet:

The External Affairs & Partnership Manager will lead all government relations, stakeholder engagement, and strategic partnerships for Meranti Green Steel in Oman. This role is critical in positioning the company as a key player in Oman’s industrial and energy transition ecosyst
data.jd_text:
**Purpose/ Mission:**
The External Affairs & Partnership Manager will lead all government relations, stakeholder engagement, and strategic partnerships for Meranti Green Steel in Oman. This role is critical in positioning the company as a key player in Oman’s industrial and energy transition ecosystem, while securing strong relationships with government entities, regulators, investors, and strategic partners. The successful candidate will act as the company’s “external bridge,” ensuring alignment with national priorities, facilitating approvals, and unlocking long-term value through partnerships across the green steel, hydrogen, and energy value chain.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities:**
**Government & Regulatory Affairs**

* Develop and execute a comprehensive government engagement strategy
* Build and maintain strong relationships with:
* Ministries (Energy, Commerce & Industry, Environment, Investment)
* Regulatory authorities and free zone entities
* Support:
* Licensing, approvals, and policy alignment
* Advocacy on regulatory and policy matters
* Monitor and analyze
* Policy developments impacting green steel, hydrogen, and industrial sectors

**Strategic Partnerships & Ecosystem Development**

* Identify, develop, and manage strategic partnerships across:
* Energy suppliers (renewables, hydrogen)
* Offtakers and industrial customers
* Infrastructure and logistics providers
* Lead negotiations and structuring of:
* MoUs / partnership agreements
* Strategic collaboration frameworks
* Support development of an integrated green steel ecosystem in Oman

**Stakeholder Engagement & Positioning**

* Develop and implement a stakeholder engagement plan:
* Government
* Investors
* Local communities
* Industry associations
* Position Meranti Green Steel as a thought leader in:
* Decarbonization
* Green industrial development
* Represent the company in:
* Conferences, forums, and high-level meetings

**Corporate Affairs & Communications (Strategic Layer)**

* Work with internal teams to align:
* External messaging
* Public positioning
* Support:
* Strategic communications
* ESG and sustainability narratives
* Ensure consistency of messaging with national and international stakeholders

**Project Development Support**

* Support project milestones by:
* Facilitating government coordination
* Accelerating approvals and stakeholder alignment
* Act as a bridge between:
* Internal teams (legal, project, commercial)
* External stakeholders

**Support the Director Operations in all relevant tasks where requested.**
**Requirements/ Skills/ Experience:**

* 10–15+ years of experience in:
* Government relations
* External affairs
* Strategic partnerships
* Strong experience in:
* Large-scale industrial, energy, or infrastructure projects
* Deep understanding of:
* Oman / GCC regulatory and business environment

**Established network with government and industry stakeholders in Oman is highly preferred**
**Required Education/ Qualification/ Training:**
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in: Business, Economics, Political Science, International Relations, or related field
  
  
**Required Language Proficiency:**
English - Fluent

data.title: Software Engineering Director
data.raw_snippet:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
  
  
Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together u
data.jd_text:
This is the general track for Engineering Director at Canonical, apply here if you are confident to run a project which spans teams and time zones, hiring and managing engineering managers and staff engineers.
  
  
Canonical's largest software products are built by multiple teams working together under a single director of engineering. Once you have passed the first round of interviews we will discuss specific software products which would be a good fit for your interests.
  
  
We believe that open source is just starting to transform the tech sector and enterprise compute. Our goal is to make open source easier, more reliable and more secure for deployment and development. We strive to be the provider of 'most software to most companies'. To deliver on that ambition, our engineers are carefully selected from the applicants across the globe. We select for brilliance and motivation to take open source to the next level. Our engineering directors set the pace and the direction to help teams achieve more than they realised they could, and feel proud of the result.
  
  
Even though this is a senior management position, managing managers, we expect Engineering Directors to be outstanding developers with the confidence to lead by example across the full range of activities in their teams. You need to understand what great code looks like in the language and genre of your product - whether that is a web service, or a system daemon, or a desktop application, or a distributed system. You'll need to know how to drive testing and benchmarking to improve the clarity in your teams around quality and performance. You'll also need to be confident to set and defend high expectations of work ethic, consistency, focus and learning.
  
  
We have director level roles across a wide range of engineering domains, including:
  
  

* Python and Golang
* C / C++ / Rust
* Data infrastructure
* HTML / CSS / JavaScript / Typescript / React
* Flutter
* Distro packaging and systems
* SAAS and web microservices
* Kernel
* Servers
* Graphics, Browser and Desktop
* Silicon enablement and embedded devices
* Product Security

If your domain of expertise isn't listed above, yet you feel it's relevant to Canonical, then feel free to apply anyway. We will route you to the most suitable team.
  
  
Location: The role is remote, with positions available in all time zones. It includes a requirement for global travel for twice per year, for trips up to two weeks long.
  
  
What you'll do
  
  

* Lead multiple teams of engineers, ranging from graduate to senior
* Develop your engineering managers and maintain Canonical culture
* Hire staff level engineers for specific roles like performance and quality
* Coach, mentor, and offer career development feedback
* Identify and measure indicators of team health and productivity
* Ensure rounded delivery including community participation and docs
* Implement disciplined engineering processes and defend them
* Set and measure progress against benchmark goals for speed and efficiency
* Represent your product to stakeholders, partners, and customers
* Develop and evangelise great engineering and organisational practices
* Plan and manage progress on agreed goals and projects
* Be an active part of the leadership team, collaborating with other leaders
* Collaborate with leads for related or dependent products

What we're looking for in you
  
  

* An exceptional academic track record from both high school and university
* Undergraduate degree in Computer Science or STEM, or a compelling narrative about your alternative path
* Extensive management experience and confidence to set high expectations
* Outstanding Linux based software engineering track record
* Drive and a track record of going above-and-beyond expectations
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
* Public speaking and presentation skills
* A love of developing and growing people and a track record of it
* Experience in leading, coaching and mentoring software developers
* Organised and able to ensure your team delivers timely, high quality results
* Well-organised, self-starting and able to deliver to schedule
* Professional manner interacting with colleagues, partners, and community
* You have advanced expertise in your own domain
* You are knowledgeable and passionate about software development
* You have solid experience working in an agile development environment
* You have a demonstrated drive for continual learning
* Builds trust, relationships and confidence
* Result-oriented, with a personal drive to meet commitments
* Ability to travel twice a year, for company events up to two weeks each

**Additional Skills We Value**

* Experience in a developer advocacy or community role
* Open source community engagement and leadership
* Ops and system administration experience
* Performance engineering and security experience

What we offer you
  
  
We consider geographical location, experience, and performance in shaping compensation worldwide. We revisit compensation annually (and more often for graduates and associates) to ensure we recognise outstanding performance. In addition to base pay, we offer a performance-driven annual bonus. We provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. We balance our programs to meet local needs and ensure fairness globally.
  
  

* Distributed work environment with twice-yearly team sprints in person
* Personal learning and development budget of USD 2,000 per year
* Annual compensation review
* Recognition rewards
* Annual holiday leave
* Maternity and paternity leave
* Employee Assistance Programme
* Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues
* Priority Pass, and travel upgrades for long haul company events

**About Canonical**
Canonical is a pioneering tech firm at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do. Canonical has been a remote-first company since its inception in 2004. Working here is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game.
  
  
Canonical is an equal opportunity employer
  
  
We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

data.title: Head of Custody Operations
data.raw_snippet:
A leading asset management firm based in Muscat is seeking a highly experienced
**Head of Custody Operations**
to lead and transform its custody function. This is a senior leadership role with full responsibility for overseeing custody operations, ensuring best-in-class service delivery, robust risk
data.jd_text:
A leading asset management firm based in Muscat is seeking a highly experienced
**Head of Custody Operations**
to lead and transform its custody function. This is a senior leadership role with full responsibility for overseeing custody operations, ensuring best-in-class service delivery, robust risk management, and regulatory compliance across global markets.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Operational Leadership**

* Lead and manage the custody operations function, including settlement, corporate actions, income processing, and reconciliations
* Define and execute operational strategy aligned with business objectives
* Ensure seamless daily processing of securities transactions across international markets

**Securities Settlement & Asset Servicing**

* Oversee end-to-end trade settlement across asset classes (equities, fixed income, derivatives)
* Manage corporate actions lifecycle (dividends, rights issues, mergers, stock splits, etc.)
* Ensure accurate income processing and entitlement distribution

**Risk Management & Controls**

* Establish and maintain strong operational controls to mitigate risk
* Oversee reconciliations across cash, securities, and client accounts
* Lead incident management and continuous risk improvement initiatives

**Regulatory Compliance**

* Ensure full compliance with local and international regulatory frameworks
* Maintain adherence to standards such as SWIFT messaging and client asset protection rules
* Support internal and external audits

**Client Service & Relationship Management**

* Build and maintain relationships with institutional clients (asset managers, pension funds, sovereign entities)
* Ensure high-quality service delivery and timely issue resolution
* Partner with client-facing teams to enhance overall client experience

**Technology & Process Improvement**

* Drive automation and digital transformation initiatives
* Identify opportunities to improve efficiency and reduce operational costs
* Collaborate with IT on custody platforms and system enhancements

**Team Leadership**

* Lead, mentor, and develop a team of custody professionals
* Set clear KPIs and performance standards
* Foster a culture of accountability and operational excellence

**Candidate Profile**

**Education**

* Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Banking, Business Administration, or related field
* Advanced qualifications (CFA, FRM, or similar) preferred

**Experience**

* Minimum 10 years’ experience in custody, securities services, or investment operations
* At least 5–7 years in a leadership role
* Strong expertise in settlement, corporate actions, and asset servicing

**Key Skills**

* Custody operations leadership
* Securities settlement and clearing
* Corporate actions and asset servicing
* Operational risk and controls
* Regulatory compliance
* Process improvement and automation
* Stakeholder and client relationship management

**Why Apply?**

This is a high-impact leadership role offering the opportunity to shape and modernize a critical function within a growing asset management business in the region. You will play a key role in driving operational excellence and supporting the firm’s long-term growth strategy.

data.title: Head of  BOS Integrated Services Hub 1
data.raw_snippet:
**About This Opportunity**
Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre-sa
data.jd_text:
**About This Opportunity**
Ericsson is looking for a Head of Integration Services Hub1 within Service Line (SL) Business and Operations Systems (BOS) as a key leadership role part of BOS Delivery Excellence, responsible for leading the service excellence. The role focuses on services business pre-sales and overlooks integrated delivery models setup including System Integration, Application Development & Maintenance , and IT Managed Services.
  
  
This role reports to the Head of SL BOS Delivery Excellence for MEMA and will be part of BOS Delivery Excellence Leadership team.
  
  
What You Will Do:
  
  

* Lead BOS service excellence, managing team of senior services professionals.
* Accountable for services engagement and pre-sales including target topline and profitability.
* Responsible for aligning with Solution Area on Services GTM and drive services offering build up , presales and delivery engagements for the EMEA region.
* Secure service delivery readiness through competence planning inline with business and portfolio evolution.
* Senior People Manager of own unit and a senior Leader across matrixed organization extending to solution line professionals in other CU BOS, BOS solution lines and SD/SDU units.
* Adopt, position and expand integrated service delivery offerings; with focus on customer value realization, delivery efficiency, and adapting to different commercial models.
* Champion delivery efficiency initiatives in collaboration with program management, CU BOS SL and SD/SDU.
* Set Individual Performance and Development goals for members with regular check-ins.
* Responsible for team dimensioning; and Cost Center Responsible to deliver over-Absorption target and required Utilization/Billability.
* Drive a Knowledge Sharing and Asset building culture across their community.
* Instill a culture of add-on sales and customer value maximization.

What You Will Bring:
  
  

* 12+ years of experience in telecom industry with leadership track record in professional services including system integration, application development & maintenance, and IT managed services.
* Deep understanding of BOS portfolio including products and services.
* University degree in ICT/Engineering. A graduate degree such as an MBA is a plus.
* Strong People Manager track record leading Senior Team members and leading Leaders.
* Deep Telecom knowledge including understanding of Telecom networks, OSS & BSS components and familiarity with TmForum (eTOM, SID, TAM)
* Advanced experience with IT Systems and Integration
* OSS and BSS full stack integrations and surrounding ecosystems
* API management & microservice architecture
* Cloud-Native & Platform transformation
* Data analytics, AI/ML applications in service operations
* In depth knowledge of different SDLC methodologies including Agile, Waterfall and DevOps approaches.
* Proven experience with MS IT and ADM related frameworks – ITIL, COBIT & SAFe
* Demonstrated experience leading cross-functional highly matrixed and globally distributed teams.
* Recommended certifications (external): ITIL, Lean Six Sigma, PMP
* Recommended certifications (internal): MSCOO, ICT PD JS8 GIAP certification
* Strong consultative, presentation, and communication skills
* Proven track record to deliver results within a time sensitive and high-pressure environment.
* Highly proficient collaboration skills
* Solid ability to communicate effectively at executive levels.
* In-depth knowledge of running Organizational and Solution Level Governance.
* Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.

Note: There are a total of 4 Head of Integration Services Hub vacancies with the same responsibility and skills requirements.
  
  
Ericsson South Africa recruits in line with its Employment Equity plan and will therefore give preference to suitably qualified candidates who are members of designated groups. Would you like to take a tour to “Life at Ericsson MEA”, visit short video Click Here
  
  
**Why join Ericsson?**
At Ericsson, you´ll have an outstanding opportunity. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next.
  
  
**What happens once you apply?**
Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like.
  
  
Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more.
